elements.text: 7101.5 Transfer of records (11/19/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of Mortgage and REO files
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of portfolio records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements for Subsequent Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Transfer of Mortgage and REO files
elements.label: text

elements.text: No later than 30 days after the Effective Date of Transfer, the Transferor Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: deliver to the Transferee Servicer the following records for each Mortgage and REO for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which Servicing is transferred:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Mortgage file: The Mortgage file that the Servicer is required to maintain in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the retention requirements in Chapter 3302.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Transferee Servicer does not have the same form of document and records
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance (photographic, photostatic, microfilm, microfiche, electronic imaging,
elements.label: text

elements.text: optical disk or laser disk storage) as the Transferor Servicer, then the Transferor Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must either convert the documents and records to the form of storage used by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer or generate paper copies of all documents and records for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective for Mortgages with Note Dates on or after July 1, 2013, Servicers may no
elements.label: text

elements.text: longer make copies of original paper Mortgage file documents for any Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents (excluding the paper original Mortgage file documents specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3302.1(b), which must always be maintained and stored as paper originals) using
elements.label: text

elements.text: microfilm or microfiche. Servicers may copy original paper Mortgage file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: using scanning systems commonly used in the regular course of business at this time and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain copies of such documents as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Portable Document Format (PDF)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tagged Image File (TIF) format
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Joint Photographic Experts Group (JPEG) format or other electronic document
elements.label: text

elements.text: formats commonly used in the regular course of business at this time
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Payment history: The complete history of Mortgage payments and, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow disbursements (including the most recent Escrow analysis), with supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation, from the Origination Date of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Correspondence and reports: Copies of all correspondence with and from and reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Borrowers and, as applicable, the FHA, the VA, the RHS, the MI, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any government authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Notice of transfer: A copy of the notice to the Borrowers regarding the Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. REO history: If REO is being serviced, the complete history of receipts, expenditures
elements.label: text

elements.text: and management and marketing activities (including copies of any filed MI claims), with
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation, from the date the REO was acquired
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Transfer of portfolio records
elements.label: text

elements.text: No later than the Effective Date of Transfer, the Transferor Servicer must deliver to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer the following records for the Mortgages and REO related to the Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Notices to third parties: The notices required in Section 7101.7(b), including
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation of MI approval and commitment to insure
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Service contracts: Copies of tax and flood hazard determination service contracts, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Unpaid charges: A list of escrowed charges due and unpaid as of the Effective Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Trial balances: Trial balances, as of the close of the last Business Day immediately
elements.label: text

elements.text: preceding the Effective Date of Transfer, showing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Ownership, payoffs and other Servicing exceptions in process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escrows, Escrow advances and prepayments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Where applicable, buydown accounts and balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquencies, foreclosures, bankruptcies and REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Automatic payments: A list of Mortgages subject to automatic drafting of monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Insurance policies: A list of Mortgages showing expiration dates of the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies on the Mortgaged Premises, whether or not premiums for these policies were
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrowed by the Transferor Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Fair lending data elements: Fair lending data elements for each Mortgage as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1301.2(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Other documents: Ledger records and definitions of codes used in ledger records, trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: balances or any other documents required by Freddie Mac to be transferred to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Additional requirements for Subsequent Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Subsequent Transfer of Servicing, no later than 30 days after the Effective Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer, the Transferor Servicer must deliver to the Transferee Servicer the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents in addition to those specified in Sections 7101.5(a) and (b):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Accounts: A copy of the depository’s reconciliation, as of the close of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bank’s last business day immediately preceding the Effective Date of Transfer, for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account maintained in accordance with Chapter 8304
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac reports: Copies of all Servicing and accounting reports filed with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac for the three months immediately preceding the Effective Date of Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7101.6: Transfer of funds in Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funds and correspondence received after Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Transfer of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirement for transfer of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor Servicer must transfer all account balances (including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrows, prepayments and buydown funds) to the Transferee Servicer’s depository in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner that ensures such funds are at all times held in a custodial capacity on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in accordance with Section 8302.1(c). A final reconciliation of all monies
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to such transfer must be made by the Transferor Servicer on the Effective Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor Servicer must forward the entire balance of all Custodial Accounts related
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Escrows, buydown funds, repair accounts and replacement reserves (net of
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented advances) to the Transferee Servicer’s depository on the Effective Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Delinquent interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquent interest advanced to Freddie Mac by the Transferor Servicer as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Transfer net of prepaid interest must be reimbursed to the Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer by the Transferee Servicer no later than the date the funds are due to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Delinquent principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquent principal advanced to Freddie Mac by the Transferor Servicer as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Transfer net of prepaid principal must be reimbursed to the Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer by the Transferee Servicer no later than the Effective Date of Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Funds and correspondence received after Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within one Business Day of receipt, the Transferor Servicer must deliver to the Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer any funds for or correspondence regarding any of the transferred Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO related to the Transfer of Servicing if such funds or correspondence is received on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Effective Date of Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7101.7: Required notices to Borrowers and Third
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Parties, Borrower issues and inquiries related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfers of Servicing (12/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notice to Borrowers regarding Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notices of Transfers of Servicing to third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower issues and inquiries related to Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Notice to Borrowers regarding Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Transferor Servicer’s notice to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor Servicer must provide timely notice to the Borrowers with Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the Transfer of Servicing to ensure a smooth transition, avoid disruption in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payments and comply with applicable laws and regulations. The Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide written notice to each Borrower at least 15 days before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is due to be received by the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Transferee Servicer’s notice to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferee Servicer must provide to each Borrower written confirmation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information in the Transferor Servicer’s notice to the Borrowers within 15 days before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the date the first payment is due to be received by the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Notice requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notice must advise the Borrower of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Effective Date of Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The name and address of the Transferee Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The names and telephone numbers of the contact persons or departments of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferor Servicer and of the Transferee Servicer where the Borrowers’ inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the Transfer of Servicing should be directed. (If toll-free numbers are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available, the letter must indicate that collect calls will be accepted.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The date when the Transferor Servicer will no longer collect the Borrowers’
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments and when the Transferee Servicer will begin to collect them
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Any previously escrowed optional Mortgage life or accident and health insurance for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Transferee Servicer will not assume responsibility, with appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: suggestions or instructions for the Borrower to continue such coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Procedures for maintenance of automatic draft payments, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notice may not amend the terms of a Mortgage other than those relating to where to
elements.label: text

elements.text: send payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Notices of Transfers of Servicing to third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor Servicer must obtain the following approvals and provide the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: notices, as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain MI approval and commitment to insure as required by Section 7101.2(d)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advise all applicable property insurers, including the Federal Emergency Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agency, of the Transfer of Servicing and of the name and address of the Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to modify the Mortgage clause required by Section 4703.6
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide the required notices to the FHA, RHS and/or VA, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify all other appropriate parties, including, but not limited to, mortgage life and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: accident and health insurers, tax verification/reporting and flood zone hazard
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination services, tax authorities, condominium associations, homeowners
elements.label: text

elements.text: associations, Cooperative Corporations (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Cooperative Share Loans), fee owners for leasehold Mortgages, other
elements.label: text

elements.text: lienholders and public utilities levying assessments for which Escrow is collected
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower issues and inquiries related to Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Effective Date of Transfer, the Transferee Servicer must be able to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Respond to Borrower inquires and provide current, accurate account information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Process all Servicing and accounting transactions within five days following the Effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor and Transferee Servicers must ensure that their staff and facilities are
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequately prepared to process Servicing and accounting transactions and to respond to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower inquiries during the transfer transition period, which may extend beyond the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any Servicing or accounting problem cannot be resolved without the involvement of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferor Servicer, the Transferee Servicer, and not the Borrower, should initiate the contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Transferor Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor and Transferee Servicers must make reasonable efforts to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resolve disputes to the Borrowers’ satisfaction when such disputes arise from legitimate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower misunderstanding of instructions in the notice of Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waive late charges and, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Make appropriate adjustments to payment and credit records made for misapplied or
elements.label: text

elements.text: unapplied payments due to the Transferee Servicer but received by the Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7102:Voluntary Partial Cancellation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (VPC) of Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7102.1: Definitions, eligibility and applicability for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Voluntary Partial Cancellation (VPC) Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Key terms and definitions for Voluntary Partial Cancellation (VPC) Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ VPC Transfers of Servicing eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ VPC Transfer of Servicing and Flow VPC Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Key terms and definitions for VPC Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this Chapter 7102, the following terms have the prescribed meaning as set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Exhibit 30, Voluntary Partial Cancellation of Servicing Contract Rights Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (RPL & NPL) Incorporated Provisions, and Exhibit 31, Flow Voluntary Partial Cancellation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicing Contract Rights Agreement Incorporated Provisions, as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Flow VPC Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Flow VPC Transfer(s) of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ VPC Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ VPC Transfer(s) of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) VPC Transfers of Servicing eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Chapter 7102 is not a general offering and is only available to specific Servicers. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7102 does not apply unless and until Freddie Mac and the Servicer execute and
elements.label: text

elements.text: deliver a VPC Agreement or a Flow VPC Agreement, as applicable, in form and substance
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable to Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute discretion implementing these terms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) VPC Transfer of Servicing and Flow VPC Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) VPC Transfer of Servicing Consent and Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac consents to a VPC Transfer of Servicing, it will indicate its consent only
elements.label: text

elements.text: by executing a VPC Agreement, which must also be executed by the Servicer, in form
elements.label: text

elements.text: and substance acceptable to Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All VPC Agreements must include the following language:
elements.label: text

elements.text: “This VPC Agreement incorporates the provisions of Chapter 7102 and the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 30 by reference as a substantive contractual part of this VPC Agreement such that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer expressly agrees that (i) Chapter 7102 is operative as to the Servicer and (ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer is bound by the terms and conditions set forth in Chapter 7102 and Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Flow VPC Transfer of Servicing Agreement requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac consents to a Flow VPC Transfer of Servicing, it will indicate its consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: only by executing a Flow VPC Agreement, which must also be executed by the Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in form and substance acceptable to Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Flow VPC Agreements must include the following language:
elements.label: text

elements.text: “This Flow VPC Agreement incorporates the provisions of Chapter 7102 and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of Exhibit 31 by reference as a substantive contractual part of this Flow VPC
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement such that the Servicer expressly agrees that (i) Chapter 7102 is operative as to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer and (ii) the Servicer is bound by the terms and conditions set forth in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7102 and Exhibit 31.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8101: General Freddie Mac Servicing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8101.1: Servicer’s general responsibilities (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s general responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer to perform its obligations and duties at its own expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer as independent contractor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer agrees to indemnify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer’s general responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible for acting in the most timely, efficient and responsible manner to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect Freddie Mac’s interests. Freddie Mac expects the Servicer’s facilities and practices to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be sufficient to safeguard Freddie Mac’s interests and expects the Servicer to provide proper
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounting and prompt response to the needs of both Freddie Mac and the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees that its duties include, but are not limited to, the payment or verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s payment of the following, as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ground rents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium/homeowners association or Cooperative Corporation assessments (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for Cooperative Share Loans) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Utility assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charges, fines and impositions attributable to the property or to the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to represent and defend Freddie Mac’s interest in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s) or REO to the same extent it would represent and defend its own interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer warrants that it will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain adequate facilities and experienced staff
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take all actions necessary to ensure that the Mortgages and REO in which Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has an interest are serviced in accordance with the Purchase Documents, including this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide as amended or supplemented from time to time, any applicable law, any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA/VA/RHS/MI regulation or requirement and any instructions issued by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a business continuity plan in place that meets the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.3 to ensure its ongoing ability to conduct business operations and maintain or
elements.label: text

elements.text: restore Freddie Mac Mortgage files and records in the event of a disaster or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: interruption to business operations and processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a disaster affecting or interrupting the Servicer’s operations, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac within 24 hours of the disaster by calling Customer Service at 800-
elements.label: text

elements.text: FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Exhibit 52, Federal Disaster Assistance, for more information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To assist with Servicing activities, a number of Servicing tools are available through Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s website at https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/servicing/overview. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: tools are accessible using a secure User ID and password that a Servicer may request on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools, for a list of available Servicing tools.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) The Servicer to perform its obligations and duties at its own expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible for performing the obligations and duties required under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract at its own expense and without cost or charge to Freddie Mac unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: expressly provided for otherwise in the Servicing Contract. Any costs or expenses incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer may be charged or collected from the Borrower only to the extent allowed
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the Security Instrument, the Servicing Contract and any applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicer as independent contractor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under the Purchase Documents, the Servicer contracts with Freddie Mac as an independent
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractor to service Mortgages for Freddie Mac. The Servicer is not Freddie Mac’s agent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Servicer agrees to indemnify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must indemnify Freddie Mac for and hold it harmless from any loss, damage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses (including court costs and attorney fees) that Freddie Mac sustains as a direct or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indirect result of any failure on the Servicer’s part to perform its services, duties and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8101.2: Servicer’s limited power of attorney, execution
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: of documents (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s execution of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer’s limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to accept a limited power of attorney from Freddie Mac empowering the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to perform all duties of the mortgagee in canceling the Note and Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon satisfaction of the Borrower’s debt. Freddie Mac may, at its discretion, provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: broader power of attorney, which the Servicer agrees to accept.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with all local recording requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assume responsibility for any recording fee assessed by the applicable authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify to Freddie Mac, if requested, all jurisdictions where Freddie Mac’s power of
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney has been recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: A sample limited power of attorney available from Freddie Mac, which outlines the types of
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents a Servicer can execute, endorse and acknowledge in Freddie Mac’s name, is
elements.label: text

elements.text: available in Exhibit 53, Limited Power of Attorney.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer’s execution of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: All requests for Freddie Mac execution of documents must be submitted to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 9). The documents that require Freddie Mac execution must be submitted with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Request for Assistance Form, which is available at:
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/pdf/fact-sheet/sats_instructions.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will return the executed documents after verifying that the requesting Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: has met all conditions as necessitated by the type of action requested and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should not submit documents for Freddie Mac’s signature that they are authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: to sign under the limited power of attorney from Freddie Mac. They may execute the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following documents in Freddie Mac’s name when the Mortgage has been assigned to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Release documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assignments of the Security Instrument for initiating foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certain other documents outlined in Exhibit 53
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may request a limited power of attorney in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8101.2(a) by contacting Freddie Mac (see Directory 9). In addition, under certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, Freddie Mac may require that a Servicer obtain a limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: from Freddie Mac and use it to complete specific transactions according to the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 8101.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8101.3: Governing law (10/08/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Each Purchase Document or other contract for the Servicing of Mortgages must be construed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the rights and obligations of the parties thereunder determined according to the laws of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: United States. The laws of the State of New York are deemed to reflect the laws of the United
elements.label: text

elements.text: States when there is no applicable precedent and when to do so would not frustrate any provision
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Purchase Document or other contracts for Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8101.4: Borrower inquiries, Servicing error resolution,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: audits and access to Mortgage records, Treatment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal and property information (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Responding to Borrower inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Error resolution procedures related to the Servicing of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac audits and access to Mortgage records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Treatment of personal and property information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Responding to Borrower inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of whether a Mortgage is securitized or held by Freddie Mac, Servicers receive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payments from Borrowers on behalf of and remit such payments to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As such, a Seller/Servicer should respond promptly to a Borrower’s inquiry and must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower with accurate information about the Borrower’s Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower inquires about the ownership or assignee of his or her Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer may inform the Borrower that Freddie Mac owns the Mortgage once
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s identity is verified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller/Servicer is unable, in a telephone conversation, to verify that it is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower who is requesting the information, the Borrower’s request for information must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be made in writing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If requested, the Seller/Servicer may also provide the Borrower with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: address and telephone number and the Freddie Mac loan number associated with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Mortgage, and the Seller/Servicer must explain to the Borrower that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: services the Mortgage for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller/Servicer provides the Borrower with the requested information in a telephone
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversation, the Seller/Servicer must follow up by providing the same information to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower in writing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Freddie Mac address and telephone number that the Seller/Servicer must provide to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: FREDDIE MAC
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8200 JONES BRANCH DR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: MCLEAN, VA 22102
elements.label: text

elements.text: 800-FREDDIE
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 7101.6(b), 7101.7(a), 7101.7(c), 8101.4(d) and 8104.1(a) for additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations relating to Borrower inquiries.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Error resolution procedures related to the Servicing of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the requirements set forth in this section in resolving errors
elements.label: text

elements.text: asserted by a Borrower or Borrower’s representative relating to the Servicing of a Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must refer to Chapter 9101 for additional requirements pertaining to a Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: right to appeal a denial of a Trial Period Plan based on the Servicer’s review of the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have written policies and procedures to ensure that it reviews and
elements.label: text

elements.text: responds to any written request received from a Borrower asserting an error relating to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of the Mortgage, provided the Borrower’s written request includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information that enables the Servicer to identify the Borrower’s Mortgage file; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The error the Borrower believes has occurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: At a minimum, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a sufficient number of adequately trained staff to be able to track and respond to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any written notice received from a Borrower asserting an error in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain policies and procedures that are reasonably designed to ensure that the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigates, responds to and makes corrections in response to complaints asserted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct reasonable investigations and respond to a Borrower with either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A written notification of the correction, the effective date of the correction and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer contact information, including a telephone number for further assistance; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A statement that the Servicer has determined that no error occurred, the reasons for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer’s determination, a statement of the Borrower’s right to request the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents the Servicer used to reach its decision, information on how the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: may request those documents and contact information, including a telephone number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Respond to the Borrower’s notice of error within the time limits set forth under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Regularly review and assess the adequacy of its internal controls and procedures used in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with Servicing of Freddie Mac Mortgages to ensure compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and other Purchase Documents and applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take remedial steps, as appropriate, if any deficiencies are identified as a result of its
elements.label: text

elements.text: review of internal controls and processes or if issues are identified from a review of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s notification asserting an error. The Servicer must formally document the
elements.label: text

elements.text: results of such reviews and make the results available to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain in the Mortgage file documentation of all communication
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding any case for which the Servicer has received written notification from a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: asserting an error and provide such files or aggregated information to Freddie Mac for review
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac audits and access to Mortgage records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Freddie Mac audit rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to audit Mortgages at any time to verify the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the terms and conditions of the Purchase Documents. Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: select the Mortgages to be audited, and the Servicer must provide all related files. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: files must contain, at a minimum, all documents required by the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The audit rights contained in this Section 8101.4(c) in no way limit Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: or remedies as otherwise provided in the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Servicer’s obligation to provide access to Mortgage records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to provide Freddie Mac, upon request, access to or copies of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage records related to Mortgages it services for Freddie Mac. These records include
elements.label: text

elements.text: all documents, files, information and data, including any of the same maintained in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file or in any database or in any data processing, storage or retrieval system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (All such records, documents, files, information and data hereinafter referred to as
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Mortgage records”.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must provide access to any other records, documents, files,
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and data maintained or held by the Servicer which Freddie Mac deems
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to verify the accuracy and completeness of the Mortgage records pertaining to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Mortgage serviced by the Servicer for Freddie Mac or to ensure that the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: complying with the requirements of the Purchase Documents. Freddie Mac may request
elements.label: text

elements.text: this information via spoken or written communication.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Timelines for submitting records
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac requests Mortgage records for any reason, including audits or Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Success file reviews, the Servicer must deliver the records within 30 days from the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the request. Freddie Mac may adjust the deadline, at its sole discretion, depending on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the circumstances at the time. Failure to provide the requested Mortgage records within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the specified timeframe will be considered a violation of the Purchase Documents, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will exercise all rights and remedies available under the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Third-party responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that any Servicing Agent, service bureau or other person which
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall, at any time, process, maintain or store any Mortgage records on its behalf provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: written consent allowing Freddie Mac to access, disclose or obtain copies of all of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This includes Mortgage records related to any Mortgage serviced by the Servicer for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, as well as any additional records, documents, files, information and data
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintained or held by such other person which Freddie Mac deems necessary to verify
elements.label: text

elements.text: the accuracy and completeness of the Mortgage records or to confirm the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the Purchase Documents. Freddie Mac may request this access without
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior notice or further action required from the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Freddie Mac’s authority to act on behalf of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Guide section authorizes Freddie Mac to execute, acknowledge and deliver such
elements.label: text

elements.text: instruments and documents on the Servicer’s behalf and take all such other actions as
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be reasonably required on behalf of the Servicer, in order to effectuate the purposes
elements.label: text

elements.text: of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer also agrees to allow Freddie Mac to conduct monitoring from time to
elements.label: text

elements.text: time in order to confirm that the Seller/Servicer is satisfying its obligations under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide to safeguard Borrower nonpublic personal information pertaining to Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Treatment of personal and property information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Through its Servicing of Mortgages for Freddie Mac, the Servicer may obtain information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including fair lending data elements as described in Section 1301.2(f), concerning the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower or the Mortgaged Premises that is either not publicly available or required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: protected under applicable privacy, securities, information security, consumer protection or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other laws (collectively, “Protected Information”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer will not use Protected Information or any information derived from Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information (“derivative information”) or permit it to be used in any way that violates
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law or that represents or could be construed to represent a conflict of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: or breach of confidentiality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protected information and any derivative information may only be used for purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Mortgages for Freddie Mac and only as permitted under applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must maintain all such Protected Information and derivative information in
elements.label: text

elements.text: such a way as to ensure the security and confidentiality of the information, protect against
elements.label: text

elements.text: anticipated threats or hazards to the security or integrity of the information and protect
elements.label: text

elements.text: against unauthorized access to or use of such information while in its possession or that
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a Related Third Party, including following Freddie Mac’s termination of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such maintenance and protection obligations shall survive the termination of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service Mortgages pursuant to the Guide and shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue for as long as Servicers have possession of Mortgage files or Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Disclosure of payment history information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must disclose to the Borrower or any third party authorized in writing by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower information maintained by the Servicer concerning the Borrower’s payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history if the Borrower (or any third party authorized by the Borrower) shall request such
elements.label: text

elements.text: information in writing. Information so disclosed must be correct, complete and up to date
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must accurately reflect the Borrower’s performance in meeting payment obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: without the use of codes or abbreviations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Disclosure of material information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain information about individual Mortgages or Mortgaged Premises obtained by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer through its Servicing of Mortgages for Freddie Mac may be material to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchaser or a seller of UMBS®, MBS or WAC ARM PCs representing interests in those
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. This information is considered to be material if there is a substantial
elements.label: text

elements.text: likelihood that a reasonable investor would consider the information to be important in
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining whether to purchase or sell a UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC representing
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests in the Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may not purchase or sell such a UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclose material information relating to the UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC to a third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party for its use) without disclosing such material information to the other party to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If disclosure of such information to other parties would contravene applicable law or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations regarding disclosure of credit information, the Servicer must refrain from
elements.label: text

elements.text: trading with respect to the UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-purchase MERS® registration
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MERS System Rules of Membership – Rule 14 (“MERS Rule 14”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MERS notices regarding liens or legal actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ System-to-system reconciliation with MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MERS signing officer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deactivation of Mortgages from MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Post-purchase MERS registration
elements.label: text

elements.text: When registering a Mortgage on the MERS System after the Mortgage is sold to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, the Servicer must notify MERS that Freddie Mac is the investor and provide MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Freddie Mac loan number for the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes located in certificate of title States may not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: registered with MERS. Refer to Section 5703.4(f)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) MERS System Rules of Membership – Rule 14 (“MERS Rule 14”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that are managing “Legal Filings” as described in MERS Rule 14 on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac must provide, when applicable, the various notifications and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by MERS Rule 14 to MERS on Freddie Mac’s behalf about such “Legal Filings.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) MERS notices regarding liens or legal actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: As the mortgagee of record, MERS, rather than a Servicer, may receive notices about liens or
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal actions related to Mortgages being serviced for Freddie Mac. When these notices
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide information that enables MERS to identify the applicable Servicer, MERS will
elements.label: text

elements.text: forward the notice directly to such Servicer. However, MERS may receive some notices that
elements.label: text

elements.text: will not include enough information to enable MERS to identify either the Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: question or the applicable Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such cases, MERS will electronically notify all MERS members about any unidentified
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice(s) it has received. Each Servicer that services MERS-registered Mortgages for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac must establish procedures to ensure that it checks (on a daily basis) all electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: messages it receives from MERS to determine if the unidentified notice relates to a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced for Freddie Mac. If the Servicer is unable to determine if the notice relates to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage serviced for Freddie Mac, the Servicer must request additional information from
elements.label: text

elements.text: MERS to make such determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take appropriate and timely action based on the notice and advise MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Servicer is Servicing the Mortgage so that MERS can promptly update its records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) System-to-system reconciliation with MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must comply with all MERS-registered Mortgage loan data reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in the MERS Governing Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) MERS signing officer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must ensure that only its duly authorized officers or employees, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: appointed by MERS pursuant to a MERS corporate resolution, are permitted to act as MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: signing officers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Deactivation of Mortgages from MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may, in its discretion, “deactivate” a Mortgage from the MERS System. Upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: deactivation, the Servicer must prepare an assignment of the Mortgage from MERS to itself
elements.label: text

elements.text: in recordable form and, where required by law, record the executed assignment in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable public land records. MERS will notify Freddie Mac of the deactivation of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in which Freddie Mac has an interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8102: Servicers and Servicing Agents
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8102.1 Managing Servicing Agents (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s and Servicing Agent’s rights and responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for termination and transfer of Subservicing Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approval to use Servicing Agents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review of Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Oversight and surveillance

elements.text: (a) Servicer’s and Servicing Agent’s rights and responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law and the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service Government Mortgages and Mortgages covered by mortgage insurance in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the Servicing requirements of the Purchase Documents and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable FHA, VA, RHS or MI requirements. To the extent that those requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: may conflict with the Purchase Documents, the FHA, VA, RHS or MI requirements, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, will control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller that is not also a Servicer must enter into a Concurrent Transfer of Servicing to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer in accordance with the requirements of Chapter 7101. The Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: request approval of a Transfer of Servicing to a Transferee Servicer or designate another
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer as its Servicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicing Agent must be an approved Freddie Mac Servicer and must comply with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Servicing requirements set forth in the Guide and other applicable Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents containing Servicing requirements (e.g., Servicing provisions that amend the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide). A fully executed Form 479A, Single-Family Servicing Agent Certification and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, that has been approved and acknowledged by Freddie Mac is a Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document. The Master Servicer and the Servicing Agent agree and acknowledge that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent is only authorized to review and use Mortgage data (including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, reviewing and using data through access to any Freddie Mac Servicing Tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: available through Freddie Mac Gateway®) related to the Mortgages for which the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent has been engaged to perform the Servicing obligations of the Master Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding Freddie Mac’s written approval of a Servicing Agent, in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the provisions of this Section 8102.1, and notwithstanding the actual performance by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent of all or a portion of the Master Servicer’s Servicing responsibilities,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent and Master Servicer agree:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That they are jointly and severally liable to Freddie Mac and shall indemnify, defend and
elements.label: text

elements.text: hold Freddie Mac harmless from and against any and all loss, damage or expense,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including court costs and attorney fees arising out of or relating to the Master Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to comply with any Servicing requirement in the Guide or other applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents that is a result of, or caused by, the Servicing Agent’s failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with any Servicing requirements within the scope of its Subservicing Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: or violation of its representations, warranties, covenants and agreements set forth in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed Form 479A
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That Freddie Mac may exercise any and all rights and remedies available under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide, other applicable Purchase Documents and applicable law against either or both the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer and/or the Servicing Agent for any such Servicing violation(s), provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: that under no circumstances will Freddie Mac be entitled to any duplicative recovery. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer shall continue to be responsible and liable to Freddie Mac for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements and all representations and warranties, in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Master Servicer and its Servicing Agent agree and fully understand that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Master Servicer must disclose all relevant Purchase Documents containing Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements to its Servicing Agent prior to execution of Form 479A
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: oversee its Servicing Agent’s performance under both the Subservicing Agreement and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 479A executed by both the Master Servicer and Servicing Agent whereby the

elements.text: Servicing Agent, among other things, agreed to service the Mortgages in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing requirements of the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicing Agent’s rights under the Subservicing Agreement are subject and
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate in all respects to all rights, powers and prerogatives of Freddie Mac under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, at law and in equity. The Guide and any applicable Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document containing Servicing requirements shall supersede any inconsistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement or provision set forth in the Subservicing Agreement between the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and Subservicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicing Agent’s rights under the Subservicing Agreement are without recourse to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac of any kind whatsoever, such that the Servicing Agent’s rights to perform
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subservicing functions and to be compensated for the same (but not the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent’s indemnification rights, if any, against the Master Servicer) all pursuant to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subservicing Agreement, are subject to extinguishment at any time. Freddie Mac’s rights,
elements.label: text

elements.text: powers and prerogatives include, without limitation, Freddie Mac’s right to:

elements.text: ❑ Suspend or terminate the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: without limitation, any purported rights of first offer, rights of first refusal or other

elements.text: to Servicing the Mortgages under the applicable Purchase Documents or Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights as a third-party beneficiary of the Subservicing Agreement, the prevailing party

elements.text: shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorney fees, court costs and expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Master Servicer and Servicing Agent’s execution of Form 479A and submission of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Annual Certification Report constitutes both the Master Servicer’s and its Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent’s express written consent to permit Freddie Mac to have access to, or to have
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosed to Freddie Mac, and to receive copies of:

elements.text: ❑ Any and all Mortgage records pertaining to any Mortgage serviced by the Master

elements.text: (b) Requirements for termination and transfer of Subservicing Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Termination or expiration of Subservicing Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Master Servicer must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 3) in the event its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subservicing Agreement with an approved Servicing Agent expires or is terminated by
elements.label: text

elements.text: either party within one Business Day of such an expiration or termination by submitting a
elements.label: text

elements.text: new Form 479A. When the Form 479A is submitted, the Master Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as to whether the Master Servicer will service the designated Mortgages or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Master Servicer intends to engage a new Servicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Suspension or disqualification of the Master Servicer or termination of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac suspends or disqualifies the Master Servicer as an approved Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer or terminates the Master Servicer’s Servicing Contract and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract Rights, then, notwithstanding any provision in the Subservicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement entered into by and between the Master Servicer and an approved Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent with respect to the Servicing of Mortgages for Freddie Mac, Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: agrees that any such Subservicing Agreement shall, without notice, demand or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: action, immediately terminate and be of no further force and effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: Any purported rights of first offer, rights of first refusal or other similar rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: in favor of the Servicing Agent or any other third party with respect to, in whole or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: part, the contractual rights to service for Freddie Mac the Mortgages shall also
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediately terminate upon termination of the Subservicing Agreement and be of no
elements.label: text

elements.text: further force and effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Immediately following any such event, the Master Servicer and the Servicing Agent shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: work with Freddie Mac to transfer all Mortgage file documents and Servicing records in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Freddie Mac’s instructions and do any and all other acts that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, in its sole discretion, deems necessary to facilitate a Transfer of Servicing to
elements.label: text

elements.text: another Servicer designated by Freddie Mac. In such event, Freddie Mac, at its sole
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion, may contract separately with the Servicing Agent for the Servicing of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that the Master Servicer was previously responsible for Servicing, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent agrees to assume primary responsibility for Servicing such Mortgages in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the terms of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary in any Subservicing Agreement, in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: event of the suspension, disqualification or termination of the Servicing Agent as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Servicer, then any such Subservicing Agreement shall, without notice,
elements.label: text

elements.text: demand or other action, immediately terminate and be of no further force and effect with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to Freddie Mac Mortgages. Immediately following any such suspension,
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualification or termination, the Master Servicer and the Servicing Agent (as may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate) shall execute and transfer all such Mortgage file documents and Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: records and do all such acts as the Master Servicer may deem necessary to facilitate a

elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, provided Freddie Mac’s approval of the new Servicing Agent has been obtained

elements.text: in accordance with this Section 8102.1, of all of the Servicing Agent’s Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations with respect to the Mortgages.

elements.text: (iii) Transfer of records, document custody requirements and transfers of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whenever the performance of a Master Servicer’s Servicing obligations with respect to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or group of Mortgages is to be undertaken by a new Servicing Agent or will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: undertaken directly by a Master Servicer following the termination or expiration of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subservicing Agreement with its former Servicing Agent, the Master Servicer and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent shall comply with the requirements of Section 8102.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Master Servicer indemnification of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Servicer shall, and hereby agrees to, indemnify Freddie Mac from and against
elements.label: text

elements.text: any losses, damages, claims or expenses (including court costs and reasonable attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees) incurred by Freddie Mac as a direct or indirect consequence of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent’s bankruptcy, insolvency or failure to comply with any of the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Approval to use Servicing Agents
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Master Servicer may not engage more than one Servicing Agent for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages being serviced under a particular Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer number at any one
elements.label: text

elements.text: time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Master Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval to use a Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Servicing Agent may commence any Servicing activities on behalf of the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A Seller/Servicer that intends to engage a Servicing Agent to perform all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations on behalf of the Seller/Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval to use that Servicing Agent prior to delivery of a Mortgage to Freddie Mac that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: intends to be serviced by the Servicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, with respect to a Concurrent or Subsequent Transfer of Servicing, a Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer that intends to use a Servicing Agent with respect to the transferred Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract and Servicing Contract Rights of the related Mortgages must obtain Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: written approval before the Servicing Agent commences any Servicing activities on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferee/Master Servicer. To request approval, the Master Servicer must submit a Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 479A to Freddie Mac (see Directory 3) executed by both the Master Servicer and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent. Freddie Mac reserves the right to disallow use or rescind authorization of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Servicing Agent that it has determined does not service Mortgages in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Review of Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before submitting the executed Form 479A, the prospective Master Servicer must carefully
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the proposed Servicing Agent’s procedures, methods of operation and minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: security standards, as set forth in Sections 1401.2(c) and 1401.2(d), for compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By submitting a Form 479A and proposing to use a particular Servicing Agent, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: prospective Master Servicer shall be deemed to represent and warrant to Freddie Mac that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: has carefully reviewed the Servicing Agent’s procedures, methods of operation and minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: security standards for compliance with Freddie Mac’s requirements and has determined that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Agent has demonstrated the ability to service Mortgages in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: such requirements by having satisfactorily serviced for a reasonable period of time an
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing portfolio of Mortgages of the same types as the Mortgages to be serviced for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Oversight and surveillance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Servicer must establish an oversight and surveillance program to monitor its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent’s compliance with the Servicing requirements of the Guide and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents applicable to the Master Servicer. In establishing an oversight and
elements.label: text

elements.text: surveillance program, a Master Servicer should consider adopting the best practices set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Servicing Agent Oversight and Surveillance Program Best Practices document
elements.label: text

elements.text: published on https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/pdf/fact-sheet/master_surveillance.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Management experience of the Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Servicer must employ a seasoned Mortgage Servicing executive and experienced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Servicing staff to oversee the activities of the Servicing Agent, taking into
elements.label: text

elements.text: consideration the volume of subserviced Mortgages and the complexity of the portfolio
elements.label: text

elements.text: being serviced (e.g., non-performing high-risk Mortgages). When determining the
elements.label: text

elements.text: experience level, Master Servicers should consider the average years of experience,
elements.label: text

elements.text: education, qualifications and demonstrated ability of the employee in relation to their
elements.label: text

elements.text: respective levels of responsibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Outsourced Vendors
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not consider a Servicer’s use of an Outsourced Vendor to constitute the
elements.label: text

elements.text: use of a Servicing Agent. In addition, the use of an Outsourced Vendor does not require
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval, unless otherwise provided in the Guide or the Servicer’s other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents. Without limiting the Servicer’s responsibilities under Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2401.1(e) with respect to Related Third Parties, the Servicer must ensure that the Outsourced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vendor has:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Systems and software applications that function accurately and in compliance with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Systems security that complies with Chapter 1302, industry standards and all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: privacy and data breach laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A business continuity plan in place, consistent with Section 1302.3, to maintain and
elements.label: text

elements.text: restore the information within a pre-determined time frame for all Mortgages serviced for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in the event of a disaster or other interruption of business operations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Processes and procedures to ensure prompt and accurate responses to Borrowers. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer that uses an Outsourced Vendor domiciled in, or who provides services to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer from, an offshore location (i.e., not in any State) must apply the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements and ensure the same level of service and compliance that is applicable to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Outsourced Vendor domiciled in and providing services to the Servicer from a State.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Both the Master Servicer and its Servicing Agent must ensure that a Servicing Agent’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Outsourced Vendor complies with the Servicing requirements in the Guide and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The use of an Outsourced Vendor does not relieve the Servicer of any of Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities to Freddie Mac under the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer shall continue to be responsible and liable to Freddie Mac for all the actions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: omissions of any Outsourced Vendor used by the Servicer in accordance with the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents.

elements.text: ■ Servicing obligations undertaken by new Servicing Agent or by the Master Servicer
