elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing obligations undertaken by new Servicing Agent or by the Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whenever the Master Servicer’s Servicing obligations are undertaken by a new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent (e.g., due to the termination or expiration of the Subservicing Agreement with a prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent) or performed directly by the Master Servicer itself, the Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and, if applicable, the new Servicing Agent must complete and submit (see Directory 3) the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following information to Freddie Mac at least 30 days before the date the new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent commences Servicing of the Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fully executed Form 479A, Single-Family Servicing Agent Certification and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, signed by both the Master Servicer and, if applicable, the new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent, with a list of the specified Mortgage types and Mortgages with specified features
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the subservicing arrangement, including, but not limited to, Mortgages in
elements.label: text

elements.text: active alternatives to foreclosure and the terms of such alternatives, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A list of Mortgages that will be serviced by the new Servicing Agent or by the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer directly
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgages modified electronically
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the portfolio being transferred as part of the subservicing arrangement includes Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that have been modified electronically or subject to an Electronic Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement as defined in Chapter 1401, the Servicer that is transferring the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents and Servicing records must provide the new Servicing Agent with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A list of Mortgages with either an eModification Agreement or an Electronic Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral Agreement, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name of the repository holding the eModification Agreement or Electronic Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9205.3 for requirements governing the Home Affordable Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ProgramSM eModification Agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will indicate approval, if appropriate, by providing the signed Form 479A to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer and, if applicable, the new Servicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Denied Form 479A requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to deny a Master Servicer’s request to use a new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent or to undertake Servicing directly. Freddie Mac may provide the Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: written notice of the denial but is not obligated to provide a written notice explaining the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason for denial.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac denies a Master Servicer’s request to use a new Servicing Agent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: undertake Servicing directly, the Master Servicer agrees to work with Freddie Mac to
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify a new Servicing Agent or engage in a Subsequent Transfer of Servicing of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to a Transferee Servicer in accordance with the requirements of Chapter 7101.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any unauthorized Servicing of Freddie Mac Mortgages by a new Servicing Agent or by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer constitutes grounds for suspension or disqualification of both the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and the purported Servicing Agent as Seller/Servicers. In addition, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercise any of its other rights under the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may perform a post-transfer review following establishment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subservicing arrangement, which may include onsite visits, to determine whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer and Servicing Agent, as applicable, complied with the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and applicable Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Master Servicer and Servicing Agent reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Servicer, the former Servicing Agent and, if applicable, the new Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: must participate with each other to conduct a due diligence review on the Mortgages related
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the transfer, the transferred Mortgage files, Servicing data and records, Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (if applicable), obligations under the Purchase Documents and any alternative to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: and provide to the Servicer that will perform the Servicing obligations with any missing data,
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents or other information necessary for that Servicer to perform its obligations in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the Guide, applicable Purchase Documents, terms of the Mortgage and
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable alternatives to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Servicer to perform the Servicing obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer that will perform the Servicing obligations must perform a due diligence review
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Mortgages it will service and on any related Custodial Accounts and be aware of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Purchase Documents and any alternative to foreclosure, whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered and outstanding or currently in place with a Borrower. The Servicer that will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: performing the Servicing obligations must ensure that its due diligence efforts include, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not limited to, a review of the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reports and data that confirm and support information provided to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pertinent reports prepared by internal or external auditors, including any recently
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed Freddie Mac audit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request, the Master Servicer and any Servicing Agent must provide to Freddie Mac the
elements.label: text

elements.text: post-transfer review results.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Servicing reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Beginning with reports due for the reporting cycle immediately following the day the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertakes the performance of the Servicing obligations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: all Servicing reports, whether required to be submitted electronically or by paper copy, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be submitted in the name and Seller/Servicer number of the Master Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Transfer of Mortgage and REO files
elements.label: text

elements.text: No later than 30 days after the new Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertakes the
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance of the Servicing obligations, the Servicer that was performing the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations must deliver to the new Master Servicer, or, if applicable, the new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent, the following records for each Mortgage and REO for which Servicing is transferred:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Mortgage file: The Mortgage file that the Servicer is required to maintain in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Chapter 3302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer that was performing the Servicing obligations does not have the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: form of document and records maintenance (such as photographic, photostatic,
elements.label: text

elements.text: microfilm, microfiche, electronic imaging, optical disk or laser disk storage) as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: new Servicer Agent or Master Servicer, then the former Servicer must either convert
elements.label: text

elements.text: the documents and records to the form of storage used by the new Servicer Agent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer or generate paper copies of all documents and records for the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Agent or Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Effective for Mortgages with Note Dates on or after July 1, 2013, Servicers may no
elements.label: text

elements.text: longer make copies of original paper Mortgage file documents for any Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents (excluding the paper original Mortgage file documents specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3302.1(b), which must always be maintained and stored as paper originals)
elements.label: text

elements.text: using microfilm or microfiche. Servicers may copy original paper Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents using scanning systems commonly used in the regular course of business
elements.label: text

elements.text: at this time and maintain copies of such documents as Portable Document Format
elements.label: text

elements.text: (PDF), Tagged Image File (TIF) format, Joint Photographic Experts Group (JPEG)
elements.label: text

elements.text: format or other electronic document formats commonly used in the regular course of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business at this time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Payment history: The complete history of Mortgage payments and, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow disbursements (including the most recent Escrow analysis), with supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation, from the Origination Date of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Correspondence and reports: Copies of all correspondence with and reports to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers and, as applicable, the FHA, VA, RHS, MIs; Freddie Mac and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: government authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Notice of transfer: A copy of the notice to the Borrowers regarding the change in entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: performing the Servicing obligations (e.g., change of payment address)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. REO history: If REO is being serviced, the complete history of receipts, expenditures
elements.label: text

elements.text: and management and marketing activities (including copies of any filed MI claims) with
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation from the date the REO was acquired
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Transfer of portfolio records
elements.label: text

elements.text: No later than the date the new Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertakes the
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance of the Servicing obligations, the Servicer must deliver to the new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent or Master Servicer that will perform the Servicing obligations the following records
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the Mortgages and REO that will be serviced by such Servicing Agent or Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Service contracts: Copies of tax and flood hazard determination service contracts, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Unpaid charges: A list of Escrowed charges due and unpaid as of the date the new

elements.text: Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertakes the performance of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Trial balances: Trial balances, as of the close of the last Business Day immediately
elements.label: text

elements.text: preceding the date on which the performance of Servicing obligations will transition to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new Servicing Agent or back to the Master Servicer, showing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Ownership, payoffs and other Servicing exceptions in process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escrows, Escrow advances and prepayments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Where applicable, buydown accounts and balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquencies, foreclosures, bankruptcies and REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Automatic payments: A list of Mortgages subject to automatic drafting of monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments along with Borrower authorization and all necessary information to continue,
elements.label: text

elements.text: without interruption, electronic payments on the Borrower’s Mortgage, to the extent
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Insurance policies: A list of Mortgages showing expiration dates of the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies on the Mortgaged Premises, whether or not premiums for these policies were
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrowed
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Other documents: Ledger records and definitions of codes used in ledger records, trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: balances or any other documents required by Freddie Mac to be transferred to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent or Master Servicer that is undertaking the performance of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custodial Accounts (if applicable): A copy of the depository institution’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation, as of the close of the bank’s last business day immediately preceding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the date on which the performance of Servicing obligations will transition to the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent or back to the Master Servicer, for each Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintained in accordance with Chapter 8304
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac reports: Copies of all Servicing and accounting reports filed with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for the three months immediately preceding the date on which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance of Servicing obligations will transition to the new Servicing Agent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: back to the Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Notices to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer that is ceasing performance of the Servicing obligations must provide timely
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice to the Borrowers to ensure a smooth transition, avoid disruption in Mortgage payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: and otherwise comply with applicable laws and regulations. The Servicer must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: written notice to each Borrower at least 15 days before the first payment is due to be received
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer Agent or Master Servicer that is undertaking the performance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Master Servicer’s or new Servicing Agent’s notice to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Servicer or its new Servicing Agent, whichever is undertaking the performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Servicing obligations, must provide to each Borrower written confirmation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information in the notice to the Borrowers provided by the Servicer that is ceasing
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance of the Servicing obligations within 15 days before the date the first payment is
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to be received by the Master Servicer or its Servicing Agent, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Notice requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notice must advise the Borrower of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The date the new Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertakes the performance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The name and address of the Servicer undertaking the performance of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The names and telephone numbers of the contact persons or departments where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers’ inquiries relating to the transfer should be directed. (If toll-free numbers are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not available, the letter must indicate that collect calls will be accepted.) Such names and
elements.label: text

elements.text: telephone numbers must be provided for the party previously performing the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations as well as the new Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertaking the
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance of the Servicing obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The date when the party previously performing the Servicing obligation will no longer
elements.label: text

elements.text: collect the Borrowers’ payments and when the new Servicing Agent or Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: undertaking the performance of the Servicing obligations will begin to collect them
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Procedures for maintenance of automatic draft payments, if applicable. Every effort must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be made to continue, without interruption, electronic payments on the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, to the extent permitted by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notice may not amend the terms of a Mortgage other than those relating to where to send
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Servicer and any Servicing Agent must ensure that their staff and facilities are
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequately prepared to process Servicing and accounting transactions and to respond to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower inquiries during the transfer transition period. The Servicing Agent or Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer undertaking the performance of the Servicing obligations must assume
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibility for responding to Borrower inquiries received after the date of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: undertaking. If any Servicing or accounting problem cannot be resolved without the
elements.label: text

elements.text: involvement of the Servicer that was performing the Servicing obligations, the new Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent or Master Servicer undertaking the performance of the Servicing obligations, and not
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower, should initiate the contact with the prior Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the transfer transition period, the Servicers involved must make reasonable efforts to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolve disputes to the Borrowers’ satisfaction when such disputes arise from legitimate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower misunderstanding of instructions in the notices to the Borrower. Late charges must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be waived and, if applicable, appropriate adjustments to payment and credit records made for
elements.label: text

elements.text: misapplied or unapplied payments due to a payment received by the Servicer that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: performing the Servicing obligation instead of the new Servicing Agent or Master Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: undertaking the performance of the Servicing obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within one day of receipt, the Servicer that was performing the Servicing obligations must
elements.label: text

elements.text: deliver to the Servicing Agent or Master Servicer undertaking the performance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations any funds for, or correspondence regarding, any of the Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO related to the transfer that are received on or after the date the Servicing Agent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer undertakes the performance of the Servicing obligations.

elements.text: Chapter 8103: Servicer Accounting and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Application of Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8103.1: Servicer fiscal responsibilities (07/09/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Balance sheet and financial statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage Servicing Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Balance sheet and financial statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must prepare its balance sheet and other financial statements that clearly reflect
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale of Mortgages to Freddie Mac as a sale of assets. This is required in addition to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: file identification and marking of accounting records required elsewhere in this Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgage Servicing Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer acknowledges and agrees that any reference to a “mortgage servicing right” or
elements.label: text

elements.text: “MSR” in its books and records or in any other third-party contract related to the Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages and/or related to the compensation Freddie Mac pays or may
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay in the future to such Servicer (i.e., in the form of a Servicing Spread) is in fact a short￾hand reference for the term “Servicing Contract Right”.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although some participants in the mortgage industry refer to MSRs and mortgage servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights, initially used as accounting terms, instead of the more appropriate and complete term
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Servicing Contract Rights”, for precision, Freddie Mac generally, and the provisions of our
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, will continue to use the term “Servicing Contract Rights”.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding any extra-contractual meanings associated with the terms “MSR” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: “mortgage servicing rights”, the terms “MSR” and “Mortgage Servicing Rights” (and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: references to ownership thereof by any Servicer): (i) are for convenience purposes only as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of industry and accounting convention (e.g., pursuant to generally accepted accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: principles), and (ii) in fact refer to Servicing Contract Rights (1) that are conditional in nature
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., may be terminated in whole or in part by Freddie Mac in its unfettered discretion) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (2) are not owned by the Servicer, but rather with respect to which a Servicer may have rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient to satisfy UCC § 9-203(b)(2).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8103.2: Mortgage accounting records, methods and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: accounting with respect to delinquency relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage accounting records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting with respect to delinquency relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgage accounting records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Permanent records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain permanent Mortgage accounting records for each Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold to Freddie Mac. The records must contain the complete Freddie Mac nine-digit loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: number assigned to the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Accounting system
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires that the Servicer’s Mortgage accounting system be able to produce
elements.label: text

elements.text: an account transcript for each Mortgage, itemizing the following in chronological order:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The date, amount and breakdown of principal, interest and Negative Amortization of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The date to which interest is paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The date, amount and nature of each disbursement, advance, adjustment or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions affecting the amounts due from or to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The date, amount and nature of each disbursement, advance, adjustment or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction affecting the amounts due from or to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The system must also be capable of providing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current outstanding principal balance and Negative Amortization of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current Escrow (impound) balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disclosure of any insufficiency in Escrow balances for a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Accounting principles
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain the accounts and records for Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to sound and generally accepted accounting principles in a manner that permits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s representatives or designees to examine and audit these accounts and
elements.label: text

elements.text: records at any time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Accounting methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Amortization method
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must use the amortization method of individual loan accounting, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest calculated in arrears, for each Mortgage it services for Freddie Mac. Under this
elements.label: text

elements.text: method, an individual Mortgage payment is applied to interest and principal by first
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculating the interest portion and then applying the balance of the payment as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal reduction. Below is information related to the interest calculation method:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Interest calculation method
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise specifically required by law, the interest portion of the payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be determined by computing one full month’s interest on the outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal balance (see Exhibit 62, Interest Calculation: Amortization Method – Full
elements.label: text

elements.text: Month’s Interest) regardless of the day on which the payment is actually received. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the interest due for the month, multiply the outstanding principal balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the interest rate of the Mortgage and divide by 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Factors used for interest calculations must be carried to at least six decimal places,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then rounded to the nearest decimal place based on the third digit. After applying the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest portion of the payment, the remainder is applied to principal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Instruments provide for this amortization
elements.label: text

elements.text: method, unless the law of the State where the Mortgaged Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifically requires a different method. If applicable law specifically requires a
elements.label: text

elements.text: different method, the Servicer must so notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 7) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing before remitting payments to Freddie Mac. When computations involve
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiple installments, such as several delinquent installments, the interest from each
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be computed using the same principal balance (Exhibit 63, Interest Calculation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amortization Method – Multiple Installments on Same Principal Balance).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount to be applied to interest from each installment also may be calculated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: succession, using the principal balance remaining after the previous calculation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal application (Exhibit 64, Interest Calculation: Amortization Method -
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multiple Installments on Respective Principal Balances). Similarly, a method that
elements.label: text

elements.text: strictly applies payments according to a predetermined amortization schedule is also
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Payment calculation for Initial InterestSM Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Initial Interest Mortgages, the monthly payment will be interest-only followed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully amortizing principal and interest payments beginning on the First Amortizing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Date. For Initial Interest Mortgages, Servicers must have the ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: produce monthly payment statements for Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, for Initial Interest 3/1, 5/1 and 7/1 10-year Interest Only Period ARMs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must be able to track the first Interest Change Date and the First Amortizing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Date separately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Interest calculations involving a period of less than one month
elements.label: text

elements.text: For interest calculations involving a period of less than one month (e.g., Mortgages paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: in full or third-party sales), the amount of interest must be based on actual days and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: 365-day year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Interest-in-advance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not permit the use of the prepaid interest or interest-in-advance
elements.label: text

elements.text: methods on Mortgages purchased. Any Servicer using the interest-in-advance method
elements.label: text

elements.text: must convert to the interest-in-arrears method for Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: before delivering the Mortgages to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Accounting with respect to delinquency relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Servicing requirements regarding delinquency relief are described in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203. These sections should be read in conjunction with the accounting requirements in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When delinquency relief has been granted, the Servicer must apply funds collected from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower according to the governing Note or repayment agreement. Regardless of cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected from the Borrower, the Servicer must report to Freddie Mac in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: net yield reporting concept. Freddie Mac will receive its proportionate share of principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: collections and the Accounting Net Yield interest for the monthly reporting period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8103.3: Application of Mortgage payments (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General application of payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Differences in collection of payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage paid in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partial prepayments (curtailments)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reapplication of prior repayments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General application of payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as described in Sections 8103.3(b) through 8103.3(d), all payments of the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: installment due shall be applied as specified in the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Differences in collection of payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage, all payments received must equal or exceed the monthly principal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest and Escrow, if applicable, unless a deficiency of $50 or less occurs. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not create a payment deficiency by deducting a late charge from the regular monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment. Refer to Section 9102.2 for further information on late charge collections.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage payment, including Escrow amounts, that is deficient by $50 or less may be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applied by reducing the amount credited to the Escrow balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credited to an unapplied or suspense funds account until a full payment is received
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Returned to the Borrower for a complete payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the deficiency exceeds $50, the partial payment must be either credited to unapplied or
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspense funds until a full payment is received or returned to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Partial payments received from a Borrower during a repayment plan must be held in Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s Escrow Custodial Account until a full payment is received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 8302.2(b) for requirements regarding the deposit of partial payments to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Checks returned because of insufficient funds must be reflected as a complete reversal of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent Escrow, interest, Negative Amortization and principal application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Mortgages with a deferred UPB (also referred to as non-interest bearing UPB), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements within this section continue to apply. In accordance with the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement, Payment Deferral agreement and/or Section 8303.2(a), as applicable, deferred
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB is due upon the earliest of the transfer or sale of all or a portion of the property, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff of the interest-bearing UPB or the Mortgage maturity date (as modified).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage paid in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General Servicer responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must determine and accept the amount required to pay a Mortgage in full,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the interest to the payoff date and remit the total proceeds due Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible for providing payoff amounts to individual Borrowers or their
elements.label: text

elements.text: agents and is liable to Freddie Mac for any errors in the amount of any payoff.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take all actions required by law, within statutory time limits, to release
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lien and discharge the debt of a Mortgage that has been paid in full (paid off). The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer agrees to hold Freddie Mac, its successors and assigns harmless from and
elements.label: text

elements.text: against any and all losses, claims, demands, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including reasonable attorney fees) of every nature and character that may arise or be
elements.label: text

elements.text: asserted against or be incurred by Freddie Mac as a result of the Servicer’s failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is evidenced by a Security Instrument or is insured or guaranteed under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: federal program that prohibits the charging of a release or reconveyance fee upon payoff,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer may not charge such a fee even if permitted to do so under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This prohibition extends to trustees in deed of trust jurisdictions and applies to “release”
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees, “processing” fees, “forwarding” fees, “document preparation” fees and similarly
elements.label: text

elements.text: denominated charges. In deed of trust jurisdictions, a Servicer must not knowingly
elements.label: text

elements.text: employ or retain a trustee who charges Borrowers for reconveyance services.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If permitted to do so under applicable law and the Security Instrument, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge the Borrower for the actual cost of recording the release or reconveyance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must request the release of the Note and other documents held by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian in accordance with Section 8107.1 for a Mortgage that has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid in full. The Servicer must comply with any and all statutory requirements and time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frames regarding disposition of the Note, which may require returning the Note to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower following pay off. For an eMortgage (as defined in Section 1402.1(b)) that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been paid in full, the Servicer must also comply with the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff proceeds received by the Servicer must be remitted and reported to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with applicable provisions of Chapters 8303 and 8304.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Prepayment Penalty Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to a Prepayment Penalty Mortgage, the Servicer must collect the amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the prepayment penalty provided for in and under the conditions specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage loan instruments and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Prepayment Penalty Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac prior to October 14, 2005,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must not assess or collect a prepayment penalty if either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The proceeds received for the payoff of the Mortgage are from the sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The payoff of the Mortgage is received from any source, including insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds, in connection with the workout of a delinquent Mortgage or due to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: default under the terms of Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Prepayment Penalty Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac on and after October 14,
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2005, the Servicer must not assess or collect a prepayment penalty if either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The proceeds received for the payoff of the Mortgage are from the sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises and the prepayment period is more than three years, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The payoff of the Mortgage is received from any source, including insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds, in connection with the workout of a delinquent Mortgage or due to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: default under the terms of the Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) FHA and VA and Section 502 GRH Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For FHA and VA and Section 502 GRH Mortgages, any notice of prepayment or
elements.label: text

elements.text: entitlement to interest past the date of payment-in-full must be waived by the Servicer on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of Freddie Mac. Escrow balances may be either credited to the mortgagor or
elements.label: text

elements.text: returned separately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When an FHA Mortgage is paid in full, the Servicer must collect the amounts due the
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA for the adjusted prepayment premium and for pro rata earned mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: premiums. The Servicer must then complete any required notice and submit it directly to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the FHA with the required remittances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Buydown accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any funds remaining in the buydown account of a Mortgage prepaid in full must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: released according to the terms of the buydown agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For further explanation of procedures to be followed in regard to conventional,
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, VA and Section 502 GRH Mortgage payments-in-full, see Section 8303.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Partial prepayments (curtailments)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may accept partial prepayments of principal in any amount according to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the Note. Upon request by the Borrower, the monthly principal and interest (P&I)
elements.label: text

elements.text: installments may be recalculated, provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The monthly payments are current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is no extension of the Note maturity date or the maturity date calculated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with any modification agreement in force at the time of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government Mortgage or MI approval is obtained, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The rate of interest stated in the Note remains unchanged
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may determine, in its discretion and in compliance with applicable law, whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: to require:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A modification agreement executed by both the Borrower and the Servicer, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Servicer-executed modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report Loan Level Reporting exception code 91(Recast) in the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle containing the Due Date of the new Mortgage P&I monthly payment. Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report Mortgages with a step rate via the Freddie Mac Servicing Data Corrections tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) and report Loan Level Reporting exception code 91.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a prepayment(s), with the modified P&I constant, is received prior to the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage P&I monthly payment changes, the Servicer must apply the payment in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Security Instrument but not report the payment to Freddie Mac prior to the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage P&I monthly payment changes. However, if all contractual payments with the pre￾modified P&I constant have previously been reported, then the recast exception code, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified P&I constant and the modified Mortgage P&I payment may be reported in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle prior to the Due Date of the modified Mortgage P&I payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the original Security Instrument was registered with MERS®, the Servicer must execute the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement on behalf of MERS. Freddie Mac does not need to approve any
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification meeting the requirements in this Section 8103.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will update its systems with the newly modified P&I payment as set forth in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement. The Servicer must also report and remit the new payment in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Chapter 8303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer warrants that, with respect to any modification agreement completed under this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section, the modified Mortgage retains its First Lien position and is fully enforceable at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of modification throughout its modified term and during any bankruptcy or foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding involving the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s execution of any document submitted by the Servicer does not imply that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: has reviewed the document for legal adequacy. This is the Servicer’s responsibility. When
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by local law or practice, the Servicer must obtain the consent of any statutory or
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractual lienholder and binding confirmation by the title insurer that no loss in priority of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lien is incurred. The Servicer must make any necessary recordation. To the extent of local
elements.label: text

elements.text: law and practice, the Servicer may require the Borrower to reimburse the Servicer’s costs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay expenses incurred in preparing or recording the modification agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send one original modification agreement to the Document Custodian in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the timing and other requirements of Section 9206.4(b); except for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage that is modified using a Servicer-executed modification agreement, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must send such agreement to the Document Custodian within 25 days of the Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date. The Servicer must refer to Section 8103.2(b) for Freddie Mac’s accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements regarding this subject.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal curtailments can be made any Business Day of the month and must be applied no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than the day on which the curtailment is received. To determine the interest due for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: next monthly payment, multiply the UPB by the interest rate of the Mortgage and divide by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funds received in consideration of a partial release of the Mortgaged Premises or the taking
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgaged Premises in whole or in part by eminent domain will be applied as
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructed by Freddie Mac in accordance with Sections 8401.1(a) and 8401.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: For ARMs, curtailments must first be applied as a reduction of Negative Amortization
elements.label: text

elements.text: and then as a reduction to principal. Both applications must be reported as principal due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in the loan-level transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Computing interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: For computing forecasted scheduled interest due in the next Accounting Cycle, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest must be calculated on the current Accounting Cycle interest-bearing Ending
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB, after applying all principal payments (i.e., current monthly principal payment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: curtailment and prepayments) received and reported in the current Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modifications on Home Mortgages that result in reduced monthly payments due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: curtailments may be approved by the Servicer without Freddie Mac’s approval, provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The monthly payments are current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is no extension of the Note maturity date or the maturity date calculated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with any modification agreement in force at the time of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MI and Government Mortgage approval is obtained, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Note Rate remains unchanged
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The modification is reflected in a written agreement and processed in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements in Section 8103.3(d). (Note: Per Section 1402.5(b)(i), the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must update the MERS eRegistry (as defined in Section 1402.1(b)) to provide notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the modification agreement upon a recast modification of an eMortgage (as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1402.1(b)).)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as otherwise stated in the Guide or applicable Purchase Documents, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval before entering into a modification with
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Initial InterestSM Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Initial Interest Mortgages, if the Borrower makes a principal curtailment during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Only Period, then the next interest-only payment must be recalculated based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new UPB. For an Initial Interest 3/1, 5/1 and 7/1 10-year Interest Only Period ARM,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if there is a principal curtailment during the period when the interest rate has begun
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjusting annually but the monthly payments are still interest-only, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue to recalculate the next interest-only payment based on the new UPB until the
elements.label: text

elements.text: end of the Interest Only Period; after the Interest Only Period, the monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: should be recalculated in accordance with the Servicer’s standard processes for ARMs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower makes a principal curtailment during the Interest Only Period, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: next interest-only payment must be recalculated based on the new UPB. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must remit interest based on the new UPB to Freddie Mac in the Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediately following the date the Borrower made the principal curtailment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must be able to comply with the terms of the Initial Interest Note and calculate
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or recalculate) the first fully amortizing P&I payment based on the UPB after application
elements.label: text

elements.text: of any partial prepayment received on or before the last interest only payment due date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before the effective date of any change in the Borrower’s monthly payment, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must deliver or mail the Borrower a notice of such change. The notice must include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information required in the loan instruments or by applicable law and must also include
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name and address of Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Address of property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Change in monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New monthly payment amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reason for change (e.g., reduction in UPB; loan becomes fully amortizing; change in
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest rate, if an ARM)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title and telephone number of a person who will answer any questions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Mortgages with a deferred balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional requirements on the application of partial prepayments of principal on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with a deferred UPB, refer to Section 8303.2(a)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Reapplication of prior repayments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may not automatically reapply prior prepayments or accumulated prepayments
elements.label: text

elements.text: for payment of subsequent installments. Payments advanced to satisfy future installments
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be accounted for as advanced (prepaid) installments of P&I. The Servicer should
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact the Borrower if there is a question about the Borrower’s intent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8104: Special Servicing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8104.1: Special Servicing requirements (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Biweekly payment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA, VA, RHS and MI Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incomplete improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have adequate procedures in place to promptly receive and respond to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower inquiries, claims of defects and other complaints (whether or not the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifically references Article XVI Section 50(a)(6) of the Texas Constitution) received in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer receives a Borrower notification of the lender’s failure to comply, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise discovers that the lender or Servicer has failed to comply, with the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Article XVI of the Texas Constitution with respect to a Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, it must notify Freddie Mac within seven Business Days of receipt (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5) of the notification or discovery and provide Freddie Mac with a copy of the notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or related information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must cure all curable defects related to the origination of the Mortgage or, for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers in a Trial Period Plan, the terms of the Trial Period Plan, in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of Section 50(a)(6) of Article XVI of the Texas Constitution within the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: period specified in Section 50(a)(6). Refer to Section 9206.2(d) for additional notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other requirements with respect to a complaint or objection to a mortgage modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Biweekly payment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower may choose to enter into a biweekly payment plan to accelerate the reduction in
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal on a Mortgage by applying the equivalent of one or more extra monthly payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: each year, thereby reducing total interest costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Biweekly payment plans do not change the conditions and terms of the Note regarding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of monthly payments, when monthly payments are due, the application of payments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the assessment of late charges and the calculation of Delinquencies, nor do they change the
elements.label: text

elements.text: way payments are reported or remitted to Freddie Mac or the way Delinquencies are reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower asks about using a third party’s biweekly payment plan and this third party is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not a vendor who administers the plan for the Servicer, then the Servicer should advise the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conditions and terms of the Mortgage still apply and will be enforced
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower can make the extra payments directly to the Servicer without a formal plan and
elements.label: text

elements.text: without the cost and potential risks of using a third party to administer such a plan (or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may offer such a plan to the Borrower if the Servicer has such a plan available)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower will be responsible for any late charges or payment shortages if the third party
elements.label: text

elements.text: fails to make timely and sufficient payments to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower should check the type of safeguards that will be in place to protect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s money from misuse by the third party before it is transferred to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage could be subject to foreclosure action if the third party fails to remit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s payment to the Servicer and the Mortgage becomes delinquent, even if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has taken legal action to recover any payments made to the third party that
elements.label: text

elements.text: were not remitted to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer chooses to enter into a biweekly payment plan with a Borrower after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage has been sold to Freddie Mac, the Servicer must ensure that the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for the administration of the plan are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A Servicer may offer or advertise the availability of a biweekly payment plan for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in the Servicer’s portfolio, so long as the Servicer does not specifically target
elements.label: text

elements.text: the offer or advertisement to Mortgages that are serviced for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Servicer must establish the biweekly payment plan pursuant to a separate agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: between the Borrower and the Servicer. The agreement must allow for cancelation by
elements.label: text

elements.text: either the Borrower or the Servicer at any time, at which point the monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedule would be reinstated. The Borrower cannot be charged a fee for canceling the
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Any fee that is charged to the Borrower for this service must be allowable under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law and be reasonable compared to other providers’ fees in the market
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Servicer may administer the plan or contract with a third-party vendor to administer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The plan must provide that two biweekly payments equate to one monthly payment due
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the Note and that payments collected in a given month are applied as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment on the first day of the month following the month in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments were received. In any month in which a third payment is received, that payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: is applied as a principal curtailment in the month following the month in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment was received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. All biweekly principal and interest payments received from the Borrower must be placed
elements.label: text

elements.text: in an Escrow Custodial Account that meets the requirements of Chapter 8302. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer administers the plan, then the payments must be placed into the Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account no later than the first Business Day after their receipt by the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer administers the plan through a third party, then the payments must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: placed into the Escrow Custodial Account no later than the second Business Day after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date on which the third party deposits the payments into the Servicer’s payment clearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: On the payment due date, sufficient funds to equate to one monthly payment must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: posted to the Mortgage record and the funds transferred into the Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account. The Servicer may use the Escrow Custodial Account that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: established for Escrow items such as property taxes and property and mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: premiums so long as the biweekly payment funds can be identified and accounted for
elements.label: text

elements.text: separately for each Borrower using the plan, or the Servicer may set up a separate Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account that meets the requirements of Chapter 8302.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If the Borrower does not pay the third biweekly payment in a month when a third
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is due under the plan, the Borrower may not be charged a late charge or reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: as delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. The Servicer must be able to identify which Mortgages have biweekly payment plans and
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide this information to Freddie Mac if asked to do so
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) FHA, VA, RHS and MI Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with and use its best efforts to obtain compliance by the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and any transferee of the Borrower with all requirements of the FHA, VA, RHS or
elements.label: text

elements.text: MI for Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac. References to FHA, VA, RHS and MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are made elsewhere in this Guide, and some guidance is given as to the nature
elements.label: text

elements.text: of these requirements. The Servicer must ensure that all applicable FHA, VA, RHS and MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are satisfied, so that Freddie Mac receives full benefit of the FHA insurance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS guaranty, VA guaranty or mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Incomplete improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to all other Servicing requirements of the Servicing Contract, Servicers that
elements.label: text

elements.text: service any Mortgage that was permitted to be delivered to Freddie Mac prior to completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: of repairs and/or improvements (e.g., pursuant to GreenCHOICE Mortgages® in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4606 or Mortgages with Settlement Dates before completion of renovations in Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.1(b) and CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgages in 4607.1(c) or eligibility of a property
elements.label: text

elements.text: with incomplete improvements in 5601.3) must comply with the Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to completion of such repairs and/or improvements. This includes, but is not limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Processing, management and performance of draw inspections and/or maintenance and
elements.label: text

elements.text: management of disbursements of the completion escrow account (and application of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining funds)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retention of certain documentation in the Mortgage file, such as the costs of the repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or improvements, appraisal(s) and a certification of completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receipt of a completion report, including photographs of the completed items and, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, evidence that the Mortgage remains a valid First Lien on the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises in accordance with Section 4201.2, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Meeting the requirements for CHOICERenovation® Mortgages delivered pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with Settlement Dates before completion of renovations (as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4607.1(b)), maintaining the Renovation Funds in their respective Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account for Renovation Funds (as described in Section 4607.12), managing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contingency reserve requirements (as described in Section 4607.11(b)) and requesting
elements.label: text

elements.text: removal of recourse (as described in Section 4607.14)
elements.label: text

elements.text: For specific Servicing requirements related to post-delivery completion of repairs and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements, see GreenCHOICE Mortgages in Chapters 4606 and CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in 4607 and eligibility of a property with incomplete improvements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.3, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: These Servicing requirements, which may be fulfilled by the Seller in its capacity as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, are considered selling obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower for an amount exceeding the resale-restricted price; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac after acquiring title through a completed foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excess proceeds on properties subject to income-based resale restrictions are those
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds that are over and above the amount that is required to satisfy the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness, including any additional liens, claims or encumbrances, in addition to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount(s) incurred during an REO holding period if title was acquired by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: via a completed foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon satisfaction of the total indebtedness as outlined above, any excess proceeds should
elements.label: text

elements.text: be distributed as outlined below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For properties that are sold by the Borrower for an amount exceeding the resale￾restricted price agreed upon in the resale-restricted covenants:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First to the Borrower and subsidy provider for equity due as agreed upon in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resale-restricted covenants, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All remaining excess proceeds to the subsidy provider when the resale-restricted
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants include terms for excess proceeds to be distributed to the subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider

elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First to Freddie Mac for any amount(s) incurred during an REO holding period, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All remaining excess proceeds to the subsidy provider when the resale-restricted
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants include terms for excess proceeds to be distributed to the subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Servicers will not be responsible for these REO activities. For more information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8105: Servicing Compensation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8105.1: Compensation for Servicing Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compensation for Servicing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing compensation for a new Servicer pursuant to a Transfer of Servicing initiated by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing obligations related to foreclosure and bankruptcy to be performed for the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Compensation for Servicing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The compensation Freddie Mac pays to the Servicer for the performance of its duties and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Servicing Contract for each Mortgage purchased by Freddie Mac is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount by which the Note Rate exceeds the Accounting Net Yield (ANY), with a Minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spread to provide adequate compensation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer agrees that it does not retain any rights or legal ownership interest in such
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation or any portion of the interest or other amounts due or received from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower under the terms of the Mortgage. Further, under no circumstance does a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain any legal ownership of the Servicing Contract or any related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights. Freddie Mac acknowledges that a Servicer may recognize Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: as assets on its balance sheet in the form of “mortgage servicing rights” in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP); however, such recognition under GAAP
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not confer any legal ownership interest in any Servicing Contract Rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Minimum Servicing Spread is 0.250% (25 basis points) for all Home Mortgages unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents provide otherwise.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For fixed-rate Home Mortgages with Settlement Dates on or after June 3, 2019, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum Servicing Spread is 0.500%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with single-premium lender-paid mortgage insurance, the Minimum Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spread must be no less than the Minimum Servicing Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with annual- or monthly-premium lender-paid mortgage insurance, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must meet the requirements of Section 4701.2(b)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to the following Guide provisions for additional information related to the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread topic:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide provisions related to Servicing Spread requirements
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicing compensation for a new Servicer pursuant to a Transfer of Servicing initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Voluntary Partial Cancellation (VPC) of Servicing or termination of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac terminates a Servicer’s Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights, with or without cause, in whole or in part, pursuant to the terms and conditions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents, Freddie Mac reserves the right, at Freddie Mac’s discretion, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiate a new Servicing fee with the subsequent Servicer of the Mortgages related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the VPC Transfer of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Below are key requirements regarding the Servicing compensation for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages following a Transfer of Servicing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For any Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac, the Servicing compensation will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated on the entire interest-bearing UPB of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Note Rate minus the ANY is not sufficient to pay a Transferee Servicer the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required Servicing compensation plus any applicable mortgage insurance premium,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferor Servicer agrees to pay the balance of the required Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation to the Transferee Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Transferor Servicer acknowledges that its payment of Servicing compensation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferee Servicer is necessary for adequate Servicing of the Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the Guide and applicable Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Transferor Servicer also agrees that the Transferee Servicer and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: have a right of offset against principal repayments payable to the Transferor Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: in order to pay the required Servicing compensation if not otherwise done
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicing obligations related to be performed for the Servicing compensation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In consideration for the Servicing Spread, a Servicer is responsible for the performance of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: of its Servicing obligations described in the Guide and other Purchase Documents for each of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac. The Servicer’s Servicing obligations compensated
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicing Spread include, among other things, undertaking all activities required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage in the event of a foreclosure of the property or
elements.label: text
