elements.text: a bankruptcy of the Borrower, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preparing and delivering foreclosure and bankruptcy referrals to attorneys
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Providing all documents and information necessary for the attorneys to prosecute
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or bankruptcy cases (including, but not limited to, missing documents such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes, title insurance policies and Intervening Assignments)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When necessary, paying for the preparation and recordation of missing documents, such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as Intervening Assignments, necessary for the prosecution of foreclosure or bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: cases
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resolving any title issues that are the result of the Seller’s or Servicer’s action or inaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing attorneys, including but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Collecting, receiving, processing, reviewing and paying attorneys’ invoices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Supervising and providing necessary assistance to attorneys in the foreclosure and
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Making available any monitoring, management, reporting, information and document
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery processes or systems and paying the fees and costs for such processes or
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems. (Refer to Section 9501.5(a) for information on connectivity and invoice
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing systems.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continuing to work with the Borrower to resolve the Delinquency through loss mitigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Handling the bankruptcy management activities specified in Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: Nothing in the Guide is intended to prohibit a foreclosure or bankruptcy attorney from
elements.label: text

elements.text: assisting a Servicer by working with a Borrower to facilitate a reinstatement of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or loss mitigation activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8106: Servicing Reports to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Other Third Parties and the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8106.1: General requirements for Servicing-related
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: reports to Freddie Mac, third parties and the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Computer facsimiles
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submission of additional reports to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Noncompliance fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reports to credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must report regularly to Freddie Mac on Servicing activities for Freddie Mac-owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. Servicers are also required to report certain information to third parties as well as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each report and all correspondence to Freddie Mac for a particular Mortgage must reference the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer number and the Freddie Mac loan number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Computer facsimiles
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will accept computer-generated facsimiles for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any computer-generated facsimiles of these forms must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reflect the most current version of the comparable Freddie Mac form
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be in the same format as the comparable Freddie Mac form with no alterations to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: placement of the data fields and no deletions of data fields
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide at least two lines of space between detail lines
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Submission of additional reports to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit such other reports as Freddie Mac may require from time to time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Noncompliance fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failure to provide timely, complete and accurate reports (regardless of the mode of
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission or transmission) subjects the Servicer to the Servicing reporting noncompliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investor Accounting reporting and Servicing reporting noncompliance compensatory fees are
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitored and assessed separately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to change all fees and other remedies at any time and at its
elements.label: text

elements.text: sole discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Reports to credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting payment status of the Mortgage to the credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage serviced for Freddie Mac under the Home Mortgage program, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must report on a monthly basis the payment status of the Mortgage to the credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: repositories listed in Exhibit 51, Credit Repositories and Information to Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not specify a particular day of the month by which the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform the full-file reporting. Freddie Mac requires only that the reporting be performed
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a monthly basis for all Mortgages regardless of the Mortgage status. The Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: report after each month end to allow time for payment corrections, returned checks and
elements.label: text

elements.text: other adjustments to be processed. Freddie Mac will audit Servicers for compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the full-file credit reporting requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Full-file reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Full-file reporting includes Mortgages recently originated, current and delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, Mortgages liquidated through workout options or foreclosure and charge￾offs. Each credit repository will provide the Servicer with the applicable codes to use to
elements.label: text

elements.text: report each Mortgage status type.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not require reporting to a repository that does not serve the jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: for which a report must be filed. Written advice from a repository that it cannot accept a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s report for a given jurisdiction because it does not serve that jurisdiction will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient evidence to Freddie Mac that the Servicer is in compliance with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for that jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers or their representatives are permitted under the Federal Fair Credit Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Act (FCRA) to inquire about or dispute the accuracy of information in their credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: repository files. This right to inquire about or dispute the accuracy of information applies
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any Mortgage that the Servicer services for Freddie Mac, just as it does to any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness of a Borrower. A Borrower may send an inquiry or a notice of dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: concerning the accuracy of reported information about the Borrower’s Mortgage directly
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Servicer or to any of the credit repositories listed in Exhibit 51. Whenever the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer receives such an inquiry or notice of dispute from a Borrower or receives a letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: from a credit repository requesting verification or correction of Mortgage-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, the Servicer must respond in accordance with the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: FCRA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8106.2: Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Forms 1098,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ IRS Form 1098, Mortgage Interest Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ IRS Form 1099-A, Acquisition or Abandonment of Secured Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ IRS Form 1099-C, Cancellation of Debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ IRS Form 1099-MISC, Miscellaneous Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) IRS Form 1098, Mortgage Interest Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 1098 to the IRS and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower as required under Section 6050H of the Internal Revenue Code. This reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be done for each Mortgage owned in whole or in part by Freddie Mac. The following
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be provided:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s name, address and federal identification number must be reported for
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Recipient”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s name, address and Social Security number must be reported for “Payer”
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain copies of all statements and reports that Freddie Mac requires the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to provide directly to the Borrowers and the IRS in compliance with the above
elements.label: text

elements.text: section of the Internal Revenue Code and make such copies available for examination by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac upon request. The Servicer is responsible for any penalty levied by the IRS for
elements.label: text

elements.text: nonreporting or reporting of inaccurate information, as applicable, with respect to those
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements and reports which Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to provide directly to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers and the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) IRS Form 1099-A, Acquisition or Abandonment of Secured Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide IRS Form 1099-A to the IRS and the Borrower as required under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6050J of the Internal Revenue Code. This reporting must be done whenever Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or a third party acquires an interest in a property in full or partial satisfaction of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s secured debt or when Freddie Mac or the Servicer knows or has reason to know that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: property has been abandoned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purposes of filing these reports, the following definitions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac acquires an interest in Mortgaged Premises either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ On the date of the foreclosure sale or the date the Borrower’s right of redemption, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: any, expires, whichever occurs later, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ At the time a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure is recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A third party acquires an interest at the time of the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Abandonment has occurred when Freddie Mac or the Servicer has reason to know from
elements.label: text

elements.text: all the facts and circumstances concerning the status of the Mortgaged Premises that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower intended to and has permanently discarded the property from use. If a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that an abandonment has occurred and expects to commence foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings within three months, the reporting obligation generally arises at the end of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the three-month period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following events trigger the reporting requirement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac acquisition. (Freddie Mac acquires the Mortgaged Premises at a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale or by deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third-party sale. (A third party acquires the Mortgaged Premises at a foreclosure sale.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HUD, RHS or VA acquisition. (The Mortgaged Premises was acquired by HUD, RHS or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the VA.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Abandonment. (The Mortgaged Premises has been abandoned, three months have passed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and foreclosure proceedings have not begun.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A completed IRS Form 1099-A must be filed electronically with the IRS on or before March
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of the year following the calendar year in which the reportable event occurred. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also furnish the Borrower with an information statement on or before January
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of that year. The requirement for furnishing such statement to the Borrower can be
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfied by sending a completed IRS Form 1099-A to the Borrower’s last known address.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The form must include the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The form must show Freddie Mac’s name and address and include a statement that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information is being reported to the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The “account number” should include the nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number, followed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by one space and the six-digit Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 8106.2(c) for information related to when both IRS Forms 1099-A and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-C, Cancellation of Debt, may be filed as the result of a cancelation of debt in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a foreclosure or similar action in the same year for the same Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instructions for completing IRS Form 1099-A are set forth in Form 1065, Report of IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-A and Form 1099-C Filing. Servicers must comply with the IRS’s and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: various States’ requirements, as amended from time to time, for filing IRS Form 1099-A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should consult with either their tax advisors or the IRS concerning questions on
elements.label: text

elements.text: such requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must file all IRS Forms 1099-A with the IRS electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) IRS reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must file their reports with the IRS no later than March 31 of the year following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the calendar year in which the reportable event occurred. Even though a Servicer reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the IRS electronically, the Servicer is still responsible for providing a copy of the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-A to the Borrower (copy B) and to those States that require it (copy C). Copy
elements.label: text

elements.text: B must be furnished to the Borrower on or before January 31 of the year following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reportable event.
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS requirements for filing electronically are set forth in IRS Publication 1220,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Specifications for Electronic Filing of Forms 1097, 1098, 1099, 3921, 3922, 5498 and W￾2G. A Servicer may obtain this publication by downloading it from the IRS website at
elements.label: text

elements.text: www.irs.gov or by calling the IRS at 1-800-TAX-FORM (1-800-829-3676).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When filing electronically, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insert appropriate header information on the electronic report it files with the IRS in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the following record description:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for electronic report filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Field name Data description
elements.label: text

elements.text: “A” record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s taxpayer
elements.label: text

elements.text: identification number
elements.label: text

elements.text: (TIN)
elements.label: text

elements.text: First payer name line Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer shipping address 8200 Jones Branch Drive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer city, State and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ZIP CodeTM
elements.label: text

elements.text: McLean, VA 22102-3100
elements.label: text

elements.text: “B” record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s account number The nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: six-digit Seller/Servicer number, separating these
elements.label: text

elements.text: two numbers by one space
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac that the Servicer reported to the IRS electronically:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ When the report is sent to the IRS, the Servicer must submit Form 1065 to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 3)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Servicer should not send Freddie Mac copies of the report that it filed with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Correcting or voiding previously submitted IRS Forms 1099-A
elements.label: text

elements.text: To correct or void a previously submitted IRS Form 1099-A, the Servicer must refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS requirements to determine how to report either for electronic corrections or voids. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: avoid or minimize penalties that may be imposed by the IRS, the Servicer must report
elements.label: text

elements.text: corrections or voids to the IRS when an error is discovered. When corrections or voids
elements.label: text

elements.text: are submitted to the IRS, a copy of Form 1065 must be submitted to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 3). Form 1065 should indicate the number of corrected or voided IRS Forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-A submitted to the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Filing accuracy and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for completing the IRS Form 1099-A and for providing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to the IRS and to the Borrower in a timely and accurate manner. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must maintain copies of all statements and reports that Freddie Mac requires the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to provide directly to the Borrowers and the IRS in compliance with Section 6050J of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Internal Revenue Code. The Servicer must make such copies available for examination
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac upon request until Freddie Mac agrees, in writing, that such records may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be destroyed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the IRS penalizes Freddie Mac or assesses any fee for failure to produce such
elements.label: text

elements.text: information or because a Servicer failed to file a return or statement or filed an untimely,
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorrect or incomplete return or statement, the Servicer will be required to reimburse
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for all costs incurred by Freddie Mac as a result of such penalty or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment and an amount representing Freddie Mac’s total tax liability resulting from
elements.label: text

elements.text: such reimbursement. Such reimbursement will not be required if the Servicer can show
elements.label: text

elements.text: that it met the filing requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) IRS Form 1099-C, Cancellation of Debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report cancelations of Borrowers’ mortgage debt on IRS Form 1099-C, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under Section 6050P of the Internal Revenue Code, for all cancelations of mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt of $600 or more occurring on or after January 1, 2005, with respect to Mortgages owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: or guaranteed in whole or in part by Freddie Mac. IRS Form 1099-C must be filed regardless
elements.label: text

elements.text: of whether the Borrower must report the cancelation of debt as income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1065 includes instructions for completing IRS Form 1099-C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Coordination with IRS Form 1099-A
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, in the same calendar year, the Servicer cancels a debt in connection with a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: or abandonment of the Mortgaged Premises, it is not necessary to file both IRS Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-A (see Section 8106.2(b)) and IRS Form 1099-C for the same Borrower. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer will meet the filing requirement for IRS Form 1099-A by completing boxes 4, 5
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 7 on IRS Form 1099-C. However, the Servicer may complete both IRS Forms 1099-
elements.label: text

elements.text: A and 1099-C separately; in that case, the Servicer should not complete boxes 4, 5 and 7
elements.label: text

elements.text: on IRS Form 1099-C. (See Form 1065 for filing instructions for IRS Forms 1099-A and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Requesting TINs
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must make a reasonable effort to obtain the correct name and TIN of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower whose debt was canceled. If the Servicer does not obtain the TIN before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt is canceled, it must request the Borrower’s TIN. Such request must clearly notify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that the IRS requires the Borrower to furnish the TIN and that failure to furnish
elements.label: text

elements.text: such TIN subjects the Borrower to a $50 penalty imposed by the IRS. Use IRS Form W-
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9, Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification, to request the TIN.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, a Borrower is not required to certify the TIN under penalties of perjury.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Exceptions to IRS Form 1099-C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to report the following on IRS Form 1099-C:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Certain bankruptcy scenarios
elements.label: text

elements.text: Debt canceled in bankruptcy is not reported unless the debt was incurred for business
elements.label: text

elements.text: or investment purposes. Single-family Mortgages may be incurred either for personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes or for business or investment purposes. Thus, Servicers should only file IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-C for discharges of debt in bankruptcy if they are aware that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: is holding the property for investment and not as a Primary Residence or second
elements.label: text

elements.text: home, such as in the case of an Investment Property Mortgage, determined at
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination. In that case, report debt canceled for the later of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The year in which the amount of canceled debt first can be determined, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The year in which the debt is canceled in bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Interest as part of the canceled debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers do not need to include interest as part of the canceled debt in box 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if interest is reported as part of the canceled debt in box 2, Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: show the interest separately in box 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C) Nonprincipal amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Nonprincipal amounts include penalties, fines, fees and administrative costs. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: do not need to be reported.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D) Release of a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Form 1099-C need not be filed if one of the Borrowers on a Mortgage is released
elements.label: text

elements.text: as long as the remaining Borrowers remain liable for the full amount of the unpaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Guarantor or surety
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Form 1099-C need not be filed for a guarantor or surety. A guarantor is not a
elements.label: text

elements.text: debtor for purposes of IRS Form 1099-C, even if demand for payment is made to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: guarantor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Multiple Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages originated after 1994 that involve Borrowers who are jointly and
elements.label: text

elements.text: severally liable for the Mortgage, the Servicer should report the entire amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled debt on each Borrower’s IRS Form 1099-C. Multiple Borrowers are jointly
elements.label: text

elements.text: and severally liable for a debt if there is no clear and convincing evidence to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contrary. If it can be shown that joint and several liability does not exist, an IRS Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-C is required for each Borrower for whom the Servicer canceled a debt of $600
elements.label: text

elements.text: or more.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (G) Mortgages originated before 1995
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages originated before 1995, the Servicer must file IRS Form 1099-C only
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the primary (or first-named) Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (H) Multiple Borrowers who were husband and wife
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer knows or has reason to know that the multiple Borrowers were
elements.label: text

elements.text: husband and wife who were living at the same address when the debt was incurred,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Servicer has no information that these circumstances have changed, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may file only one IRS Form 1099-C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Rules to entity borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: See the instructions to Form 1065 for the application of these rules to entity borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., estates or trusts).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (J) Report definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of these reports, the following definitions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A debt may include all amounts owed, including stated principal, stated interest,
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees, penalties, administrative costs and fines. However, only stated principal is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to be reported. If accrued interest is included in the amount of the stated
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt (in box 2), then it must be reported in box 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A debt is canceled on the date an identifiable event occurs. An identifiable event
elements.label: text

elements.text: is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A discharge in bankruptcy under Title 11 of the U.S. Code (but see exceptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 8106.2(c)(iii)( above)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment making the debt unenforceable in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: receivership, foreclosure or similar proceeding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when the statute of limitations for collecting
elements.label: text

elements.text: the debt expires or when the statutory period for filing a claim or beginning a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency judgment proceeding expires. Expiration of the statute of
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitations is an identifiable event only when the Borrower’s affirmative
elements.label: text

elements.text: statute of limitations defense is upheld in a final judgment or decision of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: court and the appeal period has expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when deficiency rights were not preserved
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when Freddie Mac (or its vendor per
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9601.1(a)) makes the determination not to pursue a deficiency action
elements.label: text

elements.text: post-foreclosure and notifies the Servicer of such determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when a creditor elects foreclosure remedies
elements.label: text

elements.text: that by law end or bar the creditor’s right to collect the debt. This event
elements.label: text

elements.text: applies if collection is barred by local law after a “power of sale” in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or deed of trust is exercised.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment due to a probate or similar proceeding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A discharge of indebtedness under an agreement with the debtor to cancel the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt at less than full consideration (e.g., a short sale). Freddie Mac will advise
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer if such an agreement is reached with a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A discharge of indebtedness because of a decision or a defined policy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditor to discontinue collection activity and cancel the debt. A creditor’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined policy can be in writing or an established business practice. A practice
elements.label: text

elements.text: to stop collection activity and abandon a debt when a particular nonpayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: period expires is a defined policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Facts and circumstances indicating that a debt was not canceled include the existence
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a lien relating to the debt (up to the value of the security) or the sale or packaging
elements.label: text

elements.text: for sale of the debt by the creditor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a foreclosure sale where deficiency rights were preserved, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not initially file an IRS Form 1099-C. Freddie Mac will determine whether to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue collection of the deficiency of that Mortgage. If Freddie Mac makes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination not to pursue collection of the deficiency, Freddie Mac will notify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer in the report described below, and the Servicer must then file the IRS Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must review the 1099-C Loan Detail report, accessible via the “Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting” tile of the Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (SPP) (see Exhibit 88,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools), by the end of January annually and ensure that an IRS Form 1099-C
elements.label: text

elements.text: is filed with the IRS as required in Section 8106.2(c)(iii)(k) and is provided to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower as required in Section 8106.2(c)(iii)(L) for all Mortgages in which the debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been discharged in the prior year. This report will list all Mortgages owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed in whole or in part by Freddie Mac whereby Freddie Mac has determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: not to pursue collection of the deficiency in the prior year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To help facilitate this annual review, from February 1 to December 31 each year,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers can monitor the 1099-C Loan Detail report in the SPP, which also provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: a tentative aggregate list of Mortgages for which Freddie Mac has decided to not
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue collection of the deficiency for the current year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use this current year’s list to prepare for the required annual review
elements.label: text

elements.text: and reconcile any eventual IRS Form 1099-C filings; however, as the status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and/or the cancelation of debt may subsequently change, any Servicer that
elements.label: text

elements.text: chooses to use this current year’s list in such a manner must, as part of the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: annual review, reconcile the final report against any IRS Form 1099-C filings already
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (K) Reporting IRS Form 1099-C to the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must file IRS Forms 1099-C on Freddie Mac’s behalf. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must file all IRS Forms 1099-C with the IRS electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Publication 1220 sets forth the IRS requirements for filing electronic reports and
elements.label: text

elements.text: corrections. The Servicer can obtain this publication by downloading it from the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: website at http://www.irs.gov, or by calling 1-800-TAX-FORM (1-800-829-3676).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When filing electronically through the IRS Filing Information Returns Electronically
elements.label: text

elements.text: (FIRE) System, the Servicer must insert appropriate header information on the report
elements.label: text

elements.text: it files with the IRS in accordance with the following record descriptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS FIRE System header information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Field name Data description
elements.label: text

elements.text: “A” record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s TIN 520904874
elements.label: text

elements.text: First payer name line Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer shipping address 8200 Jones Branch Drive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer city, State and ZIP Code McLean, VA 22102-3100
elements.label: text

elements.text: “B” record
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS FIRE System header information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Field name Data description
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s account number The 9-digit Freddie Mac loan number and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6-digit Seller/Servicer number, separating
elements.label: text

elements.text: these two numbers by one space
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer must file its reports with the IRS no later than March 31 of the year
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the calendar year in which the cancelation of debt occurs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (L) Reporting to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even if a Servicer reports to the IRS electronically, the Servicer is still responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: providing a paper copy of the IRS Form 1099-C to the Borrower (copy B) by January
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of the year following the calendar year in which the cancelation of debt occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer can satisfy the requirement for furnishing such statement to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower by sending a completed IRS Form 1099-C to the Borrower’s last known
elements.label: text

elements.text: address. The form must show Freddie Mac’s name and address and include a
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement that the information is being reported to the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is also required to file IRS Form 1099-C with any State that requires this
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing in accordance with the State’s filing deadlines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (M) Notification to Freddie Mac of electronic reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac that the Servicer reported IRS Form 1099-C to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the IRS. When the electronic report is sent to the IRS, the Servicer must submit Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1065 to Freddie Mac (see Directory 3).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (N) Correcting or voiding previously submitted IRS Form 1099-C
elements.label: text

elements.text: To correct or void a previously submitted IRS Form 1099-C, the Servicer must refer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to IRS requirements to determine how to report either electronic corrections or voids.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To avoid or minimize penalties that may be imposed by the IRS, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: promptly report corrections or voids to the IRS when an error is discovered. When the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer submits corrections or voids to the IRS, the Servicer must also submit a copy
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Form 1065 to Freddie Mac (see Directory 3). The Servicer must indicate on Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1065 the number of corrected or voided IRS Forms 1099-C submitted to the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (O) Filing accuracy and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for completing the IRS Form 1099-C and for providing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to the IRS and to the Borrower in a timely and accurate manner. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must maintain copies of all statements and reports that Freddie Mac requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer to provide directly to the Borrowers and the IRS in compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6050P of the Internal Revenue Code. The Servicer must make such copies
elements.label: text

elements.text: available for examination by Freddie Mac upon request until Freddie Mac agrees, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing, that such records may be destroyed (which will be a minimum of four (4)
elements.label: text

elements.text: years).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the IRS penalizes Freddie Mac or assesses any fees for failure to produce such
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, or because the Servicer failed to file a return or statement or filed an
elements.label: text

elements.text: untimely, incorrect or incomplete return or statement, the Servicer will be required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimburse Freddie Mac for all costs incurred by Freddie Mac as a result of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: penalty or assessment and for the amount representing Freddie Mac’s total tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability resulting from such reimbursement. Freddie Mac will not require such
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement if the Servicer can show that it met the filing requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) IRS Form 1099-MISC, Miscellaneous Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should not prepare or file IRS Form 1099-MISC using Freddie Mac’s name or TIN.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer should consult with its tax advisor to review its reporting obligations with regard
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the filing of Form 1099-MISC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8107: Document Custody
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8107.1: Servicer responsibilities related to document
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: custody (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery of trailing documents to the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requests for release of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Returning documents to the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lost Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Delivery of trailing documents to the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receiving a Mortgage document that is required to be held by a Document Custodian,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as a modifying instrument or an original document from a recording office, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must promptly deliver it to the Document Custodian holding the related Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Requests for release of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may require possession of a Note to take action in conjunction with the payoff,
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, repurchase, substitution, conversion, modification or assumption of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or to take legal action, such as responding to a Borrower’s bankruptcy, bringing or defending
elements.label: text

elements.text: a lawsuit or other litigation relating to the maturity, prepayment, repurchase, substitution,
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversion, modification or assumption of a Mortgage or a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (each such bankruptcy, suit or litigation being a “Legal Action”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers often require physical possession of a Note for Legal Actions. Even if the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: needs only one document from the Note file, the Document Custodian will release the entire
elements.label: text

elements.text: file because it is important to keep all the documents comprising the Note file together.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In certain circumstances, constructive possession, which can be obtained quickly, is legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient to establish the Servicer as a “holder” of, or person entitled to enforce, the Note in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Action. “Constructive possession” describes the situation in which someone controls an
elements.label: text

elements.text: object without physically possessing it. In this context, a Servicer can control and direct a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note that is in the Document Custodian’s vault. If constructive possession is appropriate for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Action, the Servicer will automatically, immediately and conclusively be deemed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: have constructive possession of the Note from the earlier of the date that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Legal Action commences, or;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Document Custodian receives the Servicer’s request to release constructive
elements.label: text

elements.text: possession of the Note until the Legal Action is concluded
elements.label: text

elements.text: For physical possession, the Document Custodian will deliver the Note as directed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. For constructive possession, the Document Custodian will promptly contact the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer by e-mail or otherwise when Document Custodian’s tracking system has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: updated to indicate the Servicer (rather than Freddie Mac) as the “owner” or “investor” of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: related Mortgage. Upon Document Custodian’s release of the Note, the Servicer shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically, immediately and conclusively be deemed to be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The holder of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Entitled to enforce the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Duly authorized by Freddie Mac to take Legal Action in connection with Servicing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: related Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As appropriate, in physical possession of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers request and return physical and constructive possession of Notes from Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodians (including eMortgages as defined in Section 1402.1(b)) using Form 1036,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Request for Possession or Control of Documents, or its equivalent (“Form 1036,” regardless
elements.label: text

elements.text: of its format). A single form may be used to request multiple Notes if each Note is separately
elements.label: text

elements.text: listed and identified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Electronic, as defined in Section 1401.1(b), or alternative version of Form 1036 must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain all information required by the Form 1036, regardless of format. Any request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: release that is not delivered as hard copy is considered to be an Electronic Form 1036
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including a Portable Document Format (PDF) of a paper document attached to an e-mail).
elements.label: text

elements.text: To use an Electronic Form 1036, the Servicer must enter into an agreement with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian as described in Section 8107.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The procedures to obtain physical or constructive possession of a Note are described below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: To obtain physical or constructive possession of a Note from a Document Custodian other
elements.label: text

elements.text: than The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A. (“BNYM”) as Designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian, a Seller/Servicer must complete Form 1036 and deliver it to the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer no longer requires possession of the Note, it must promptly:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For physical possession, return the Note to the Document Custodian unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage was repurchased or paid in full, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For constructive possession, send notice (a copy of the original Form 1036 with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for termination of constructive possession) to the Document Custodian, which
elements.label: text

elements.text: will then update its document tracking system
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) BNYM as Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: To obtain physical or constructive possession of a Note from BNYM, a Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete and send the Form 1036 as described above or make an electronic request using
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Asset Repository and Collateral System (“ARK”). Contact BNYM for further
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on ARK (see Directory 4).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer no longer requires possession of the Note, it must promptly:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For physical possession, return the Note to BNYM unless the related Mortgage was
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchased or paid in full as described above; however, Servicers using ARK to
elements.label: text

elements.text: request a Note must use that method to return it and include a copy of the ARK￾generated 1036 Release Receipt Report, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For constructive possession, notify BNYM by sending a copy of the original Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1036 with a notice of termination of constructive possession or otherwise as
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructed by BNYM, which will then update its document tracking system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) eMortgage designated custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: In States in which the Servicer must be the holder of an eNote (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1402.1(b)) to commence foreclosure or other Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must follow the requirements of Section 1402.5(c)(v) and, if required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: produce a copy of the eNote for a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter, Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must follow prudent business practices to protect and safeguard all Notes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents while in their possession, in transit and in the possession of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel or agents. At a minimum, these practices include protection from damaging
elements.label: text

elements.text: elements, such as water and fire, identification as a Freddie Mac asset, secure storage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and tracking and segregation from unrelated documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Returning documents to the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Note is no longer needed for the reason cited on Form 1036, or when the Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Action is concluded, the Servicer must promptly, in all events within 90 days, return the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Document Custodian. Notes and related documents may be transported only by
elements.label: text

elements.text: nationally recognized commercial or bonded carrier or courier services and must be covered
elements.label: text

elements.text: by in transit insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 2202.3(b) for Seller/Servicer and Document Custodian requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Lost Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Lost Notes Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer becomes aware at any time that an original Note is lost, is missing or has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been inadvertently destroyed, the Servicer must promptly conduct a thorough and diligent
elements.label: text

elements.text: search of its premises and business records and make diligent inquiry of any party that
elements.label: text

elements.text: has had physical responsibility for the Note, such as a Document Custodian or
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure counsel. Servicer is responsible to Freddie Mac for the original Note even if it
elements.label: text

elements.text: did not have physical possession of the Note at the time of loss. If Servicer cannot locate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note after conducting the search, it must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retain written records describing the search in the Mortgage file, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Immediately send an e-mail to Freddie Mac (Directory 9) requesting Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: permission to create an LNA that meets the requirements of this Section 8107.1. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: response, Freddie Mac will issue:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  An e-mail indicating Servicer’s authority to create an LNA subject to the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: and conditions set forth in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text:  A written communication with a request for additional action
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Additional instructions for an alternate remedy, or
elements.label: text

elements.text:  A repurchase request, if the request is not granted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon Freddie Mac’s approval of the request, the Servicer will create an LNA as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in subsection (ii) below and maintain the LNA as if it were the original Note,
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that a copy of Freddie Mac’s approval of Servicer’s creation of the LNA must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be maintained with the LNA in the Note file. If a Document Custodian released an
elements.label: text

elements.text: original Note, it is not permitted to accept return of an LNA without evidence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s authorization to create the LNA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must adhere to these procedures, whether it believes that it or another party was
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for losing or destroying the Note and must maintain written policies to
elements.label: text

elements.text: implement these procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) LNA requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each LNA that Servicer creates must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify information regarding the related Mortgage, including the:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Name(s) of the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Original principal balance
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Recording information for the Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Address of the Mortgaged Premises (ZIP CodeTM not required)
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Freddie Mac loan number; and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Servicer loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that the person signing the LNA on behalf of the Servicer (“affiant”) is over
elements.label: text

elements.text: the age of 18, is competent to testify and either has independent knowledge of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: facts set forth in the LNA or has made appropriate inquiry of persons having
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledge of such facts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify Freddie Mac as owner of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Set forth the name and function of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State the basis for affiant’s assertion that the Note is lost or destroyed, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether affiant is familiar with and has access to Servicer’s business records as part
elements.label: text

elements.text: of affiant’s regular job functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verify that Servicer received the original Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State that Servicer conducted a good faith, thorough and diligent search of Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: premises and business records and has been unable to locate the original Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State that, at the time that the Note was lost or destroyed, Servicer was entitled to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforce the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State that Freddie Mac and its successors-in-interest and assignees may rely on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: LNA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have attached to it a complete copy of the Note showing all endorsements through
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification for purchase by Freddie Mac, if any
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contain the notarized signature of an employee of Servicer who is identified on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: LNA by printed or typed title; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Satisfy the requirements of applicable law to enforce the debt obligation in the State
elements.label: text

elements.text: in which the Mortgage Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each such State, Servicer must receive, and make available upon request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s inspection, a legal analysis from either Servicer’s in-house counsel or
elements.label: text

elements.text: outside counsel that the form of LNA used is as valid and enforceable as the Note would
elements.label: text

elements.text: be if it were present.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain States may require additional representations and information. For example, New
elements.label: text

elements.text: York requires information regarding the original receipt of the Note and the methodology
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicer’s good faith efforts to determine that the Note was lost.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Servicer representations and warranties regarding enforceability of the Note;
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer acknowledges that creating an LNA does not relieve Servicer of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation or warranty relating to enforceability of the Note and agrees to indemnify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac under Section 8101.1(d) for any loss, damage or expenses that arise because
elements.label: text

elements.text: the original Note is unavailable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide written testimony, witnesses or other support to prove the LNA
elements.label: text

elements.text: and enforce the debt obligation in a Legal Action, as defined in Section 8107.2, when
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested by Freddie Mac, even when Servicer no longer services the related Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and those activities support a Transferee Servicer’s efforts to enforce the debt obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: or otherwise service that Mortgage. If the Servicer has transferred servicing of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage with an LNA, Servicer must provide such support at the request of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent Transferee Servicer or Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Records and reporting of LNAs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must maintain a list of all Mortgages with LNAs created pursuant to this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8107.1 and provide the list to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) “Found” Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Note is located after an LNA has been created, Servicer shall immediately notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac by sending an e-mail to Loan_Delivery_Funding_Ops@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: and deliver the Note as directed below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac owns the Mortgage, Freddie Mac may direct Servicer and Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian to perform certain verifications, deliver the Note to the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: and execute certain documents as further described in the Document Custody Procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Handbook. After the Document Custodian accepts the Note, Freddie Mac will destroy, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: direct the destruction of, the LNA, and the Servicer’s indemnification obligations shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate without further action by any party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac no longer owns the Mortgage when the Note is found, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact the new owner for instruction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8107.2: Document Custodian’s functions and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document Custodian’s general duties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requests for release of notes and assignments to Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form imaging and retention requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lost Notes and LNAs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Document custodian’s general duties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Document Custodian must maintain custody of the Notes and assignments (collectively,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as appropriate, “Notes,” see the definition in the Glossary), in trust, for the benefit of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Storing the Notes in secure, fire-resistant facilities as required by Section 2202.1(b)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Servicer delivers supplemental or trailing documents, such as original modifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: instruments, the Document Custodian must place them with the related Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Affixing the Freddie Mac loan number to the Note, if advised that Freddie Mac requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: it, and, if the Note contains the Freddie Mac loan number, changing the Freddie Mac loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: number on a Note if advised by the Servicer that Freddie Mac has changed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac loan number for that Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complying with the requirements of this Section 8107.2 and all Tri-Party Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Requests for Release of Notes and assignments to Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may require physical or constructive possession of Notes in conjunction with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Action or for the payoff, foreclosure, repurchase, substitution, conversion,
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification or assumption of a Mortgage. Servicers must complete, sign and submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1036, Request for Possession or Control of Documents, or its equivalent, such as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Web Release Request described in Section 8107.1(b) (“Form 1036” regardless of its
elements.label: text

elements.text: format) to the Document Custodian. Absent manifest error, Document Custodians may rely
elements.label: text

elements.text: on information received from Servicers on Form 1036.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative versions of Form 1036 must contain all information required by the Form 1036,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of their format. A single form may be used to request multiple Notes if each Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: is separately listed and identified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Form 1036 is considered to be Electronic, using the definition in Section 1402.1(b), if it is
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmitted in any format other than paper, including Portable Document Formats (PDFs) of
elements.label: text

elements.text: paper documents delivered as attachments to e-mails. Before using an Electronic Form 1036,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires that the Servicer and Document Custodian have in place a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defines Electronic Signature and the type(s) of electronic transmission(s) permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ States the Document Custodian’s requirements for accepting an Electronic Signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ States the Servicer’s requirements for maintaining and controlling access to Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Clearly assigns liability when the terms of the agreement are violated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian must retain for each Servicer a list of individuals authorized to request
elements.label: text

elements.text: the release of documents electronically. The list must be dated and signed by an officer of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and contain sample signatures of the authorized individuals.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian should review and retain documentation of such review for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note file to verify its completeness and that the Note is original before releasing it to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. This enables the Document Custodian to verify that the Servicer returns all the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents, including the original documents, that were released to it.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Document delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Delivery and return of physical possession
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may require physical possession of Notes to service Mortgages for a Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Action or otherwise. Servicers must complete and sign Form 1036 and submit it to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian. See Section 8107.1(b) for information related to requests for
elements.label: text

elements.text: release of documents. Upon receipt of Servicer’s signed Form 1036, the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian shall transfer and deliver physical possession of the Note as directed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. Notes may be transported only by a nationally recognized commercial or
elements.label: text

elements.text: bonded carrier or courier service and must be covered by in transit insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 2202.1(b) and 2202.3(b) for information related to Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodians and document custody.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Note is no longer needed for the reason cited on Form 1036 or when the Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Action is concluded, the Servicer must promptly (and in any event within 90 days) return
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note to the Document Custodian. Upon receiving the Note, the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall immediately resume its physical custody, in trust, for the benefit of Freddie Mac, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in the Tri-Party Agreement, verify that all documents that were released have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been returned and update its tracking system to reflect their receipt. See Section 8107.2(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: below for instances in which the Document Custodian released an original Note and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer returns an LNA in lieu of the original Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Delivery and return of constructive possession
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete, sign and submit Form 1036 to request constructive
elements.label: text

elements.text: possession from the Document Custodian. The constructive possession will commence
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the earlier of the date on which the Document Custodian receives the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for constructive possession and the Servicer commences the Legal Action.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian maintains physical custody of the Note for the benefit of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer while the Servicer has constructive possession. For the duration of the Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Action, the Servicer shall be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In constructive possession of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The holder of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Entitled to enforce the Note, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Authorized by Freddie Mac to take Legal Action to service the related Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon notice from the Servicer that the Legal Action is concluded, the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian shall automatically and immediately update its document tracking system and
elements.label: text

elements.text: cease maintaining physical custody of the Note, in trust, for the benefit of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and resume maintaining physical custody of the Note, in trust, for the benefit of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Form imaging and retention requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian must retain Form 1036 for three months after the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is paid off, but they need not retain Form 1034E, Custodial Certification Schedule,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the Note Delivery Cover Sheet after the related Mortgages have been certified. Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: and forms may be retained as Electronic Records on the terms and conditions for maintaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: such Records set forth in Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In disposing of documents, the Document Custodian must have in place and follow
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures and use destruction methods that ensure the confidentiality of Borrowers’ private
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Lost Notes and LNAs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian may receive, either as a return of released documents or in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing or transfer of custody, an LNA rather than the original Note. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian may accept an LNA only if the Servicer delivers evidence of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s approval of the use of an LNA for that Mortgage. Acceptable evidence of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval includes a copy of a single loan exception or the text of an e-mail from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, and the approval must include specific reference to the related Mortgage, which may
elements.label: text

elements.text: appear on a list. If the Servicer does not include such evidence, the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: must contact Freddie Mac immediately at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan_Delivery_Funding_Ops@FreddieMac.com and await further instruction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian must verify that each LNA:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is an original, signed in ink by an officer of the Seller/Servicer that created the LNA and
elements.label: text

elements.text: notarized
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Includes the name of each Borrower on the Note, the original principal amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note, the Note Date and recordation information for the related Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has attached to it a complete copy of the entire missing Note, including all required
elements.label: text

elements.text: signatures and endorsements to the Servicer that created the LNA
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the LNA meets these requirements, the Document Custodian must verify the loan data
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Note copy attached to the LNA and maintain the LNA on the same basis as it would
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the LNA were an original Note. This includes observing all requirements relating to
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests for release.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If at any time an original Note is recovered as described in Section 8107.1(d)(v), the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: will deliver it to the Document Custodian, and the Document Custodian will follow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: process set forth in the Document Custody Procedures Handbook for “found Notes.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodians may contact Freddie Mac (see Directory 9) for instructions and further
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on verifying and managing LNAs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8201: Escrow
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8201.1: Escrow account management for property
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: taxes, ground rents, assessments or other charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: and waiver requirements (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annual Escrow analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waiver of Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-payment of Escrow charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escrow waiver exception requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest of escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain bills for and pay all Escrow items in accordance with the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage before the applicable penalty or termination date. The Servicer must maintain adequate
elements.label: text

elements.text: records to prove payment of all property taxes, ground rents and assessments or other charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: that, if delinquent, are or may become First Liens on the property or that if not paid would result
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the subordination of Freddie Mac’s interests, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9301.6(e) regarding expenses that may become First Liens on the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower requests, the Servicer may also collect and administer funds to pay expenses not
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided for in the Mortgage, such as life insurance on the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer does not collect Escrow or discontinues collecting Escrow, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must require that the Borrower furnish proof of payment; and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer may use other means (such as tax services) commonly employed by private
elements.label: text

elements.text: institutional mortgage investors to satisfy itself that these items have been paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Annual Escrow analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least annually, the Servicer must compute the required Escrow payment based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonable estimates of assessments and bills to determine that sufficient funds are being
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected to meet all Escrow payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Escrow surplus
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the amount held in Escrow by the Servicer, together with the future monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: installments of Escrow, exceeds the amount required to pay charges as they fall due, plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: any “cushion” permitted by applicable law, the Servicer must either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repay the excess promptly to the Borrower (if there is no default under the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Security Instrument), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit the excess to the Borrower by a reduction in monthly Escrow installments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any interest payable to the Borrower for Escrow, when required by applicable law, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other funds held by the Servicer, whether due to contractual agreement or operation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of law, must be paid by the Servicer at its own expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Escrow shortages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is unable to pay an Escrow shortage as a lump sum, then the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: must pay the shortage as part of the monthly payment (“Projected Monthly Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shortage Payments”) on the Mortgage. If the Borrower must pay Projected Monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Shortage Payments, then the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Spread the repayment of the Escrow shortage amount in equal monthly payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: over a period of not more than 60 months; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Account for any remaining unpaid amount of the Escrow shortage in any subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow analysis to ensure that the Borrower is able to continue to pay all Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage amounts over the remaining portion of either the current remaining Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage repayment period or a period up to 60 months.
elements.label: text
