elements.text: The Servicer may not accelerate or compress the remaining Escrow shortage amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: into a new Escrow payment or shorter repayment period as a result of a future Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Waiver of Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have a written policy governing the circumstances under which Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts may be waived. When a Servicer permits Escrow waivers, subject to the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents and applicable law, the Servicer’s written policies must provide that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: waiver not be based solely on the loan-to-value ratio of the Mortgage but also on whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has the financial ability to handle the lump sum payments of property taxes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance premiums and other charges described in the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may, by written notification to the Borrower and without Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval, start collecting Escrow previously waived.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Escrow account waiver must be in writing and grant the Servicer the right to resume
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection of Escrow if there is any nonpayment of the items for which Escrow had
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously been collected.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Circumstances in which Escrow may be discontinued
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must discontinue collecting Escrow when required by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If not prohibited by applicable law and the Mortgage is current, Servicers may approve
elements.label: text

elements.text: an Escrow waiver requested by the Borrower, except as stated in Section 8201.1(b)(ii), if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The unpaid principal balance for the Mortgage is less than 80% of the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraised value; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage has not been delinquent for 30 days or more at any time during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous six months
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, there is no minimum period having elapsed since the Origination Date of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage when evaluating an Escrow waiver request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Circumstances in which Escrow may not be discontinued
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may not discontinue or waive collecting Escrow on the following Mortgages if
elements.label: text

elements.text: they had an Escrow account when sold to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home or a 2- to 4-unit property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Affordable Gold® 97 Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac 100 Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Home Possible® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A HomeOne® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A HeritageOne® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, except Mortgages modified under the terms in Section 9206.1(c)(v), a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not discontinue or waive collecting Escrow on Mortgages that have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified under a Freddie Mac mortgage modification program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must follow FHA, VA, RHS or MI Escrow waiver and reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Non-payment of Escrow charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgage with an Escrow account
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the funds held in Escrow are insufficient to pay charges when due, the Servicer should
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain the necessary additional funds from the Borrower before the latest date on which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the charges may be paid prior to penalty, lapse of insurance policies, adverse impact to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage, etc. If the Servicer is unable to obtain the funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Borrower, the Servicer must pay any charges due. The Servicer may either
elements.label: text

elements.text: increase the Borrower’s next payment to cover the entire advance or schedule the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment of such advance over several months. The Servicer may not collect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: advance by deducting from one or more regular monthly Mortgage payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For delinquent Mortgages, the Servicer must continue to pay Escrow items for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following expenses:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property taxes, property insurance premiums and other charges as described in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance premiums, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must contact Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) and obtain Freddie Mac’s written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval before paying the taxing authority when federal, State or local income tax liens
elements.label: text

elements.text: would take priority over Freddie Mac’s First Lien position.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if the Mortgage is secured by a Manufactured Home and the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: becomes 60 days or more delinquent, a Servicer must start collecting Escrow that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously waived as a part of any repayment arrangement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgage without an Escrow account
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Escrow is not collected and the Servicer discovers nonpayment of any charge
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise payable from Escrow, the Servicer must contact the Borrower and allow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower 30 days to provide proof of payment. The Servicer must advance funds for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unpaid charge and any applicable penalty if the Borrower indicates inability to make the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or does not provide proof of payment within the required 30 days. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must attempt to work out an arrangement with the Borrower for repayment of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: advance and, if allowed by applicable law, must begin to collect Escrow for future bills.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If both:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower fails to pay any charge otherwise payable from Escrow; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has advanced funds for the unpaid charge and any applicable penalty
elements.label: text

elements.text: And either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A mutually satisfactory arrangement cannot be made for the Borrower’s repayment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the advance or the Borrower fails to comply with the terms of any such arrangement;
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower fails to pay Escrow to the Servicer after the Servicer starts collecting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow that was previously waived
elements.label: text

elements.text: Then the Servicer must comply with the collection, loss mitigation and, if necessary,
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure referral requirements set forth in Chapters 9101 or 9102, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Escrow waiver exception requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer believes that the Borrower should not need an Escrow account, even though
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower did not meet the requirements under Section 8201.1(c), then the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit an exception request to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Interest on Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer either has entered into an agreement or is required by law to pay interest on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow, the Servicer is solely and fully responsible for this payment. The accounting related
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the payment of interest on Escrow may not be included with the regular Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounting for principal and interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8202: Property Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8202.1: General property insurance requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General property insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum property insurance types and amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance charges covering the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General property insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For as long as Freddie Mac owns a Mortgage, the Servicer must ensure that the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is covered by insurance meeting the requirements of Chapters 4703 and 8202.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must ensure that each insurer or reinsurer that provides property or flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage for each Mortgaged Premises securing a Mortgage owned by Freddie Mac meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the minimum licensing, assessment and rating requirements outlined in Section 4703.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurance policies must include coverage for fire, windstorm, hurricane, hail, flood and
elements.label: text

elements.text: localized perils, when applicable, and provide claims to be settled based on replacement cost.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 4703.2 for requirements for minimum property insurance types and
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts and Section 4703.3 for flood insurance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the carrier, policy amount and coverage meet the minimum Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements described in Chapter 4703
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure property and flood, when applicable, insurance premiums are paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain Lender-Placed Insurance (LPI) when required in accordance with applicable law,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Security Instrument and the Guide. See Section 8202.6 for LPI requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgage clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must ensure all insurance policies documenting insurance coverage(s) obtained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Freddie Mac’s requirements for 1- to 4-unit properties include the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: industry’s standard Mortgage clause and meet the requirements outlined in Section 4703.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Minimum property insurance types and amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Freddie Mac-owned Mortgage it services, the Servicer must have policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls in place to ensure that the Mortgaged Premises is insured and the coverage meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum property insurance requirements outlined in Section 4703.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If at any time during the term of the Mortgage the Mortgaged Premises is not covered by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum property insurance requirements outlined in Section 4703.2, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: follow the LPI process under Section 8202.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Insurance charges covering the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premiums for insurance covering the Mortgaged Premises will be paid when due by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers or the Servicer if the Servicer collects Escrows for such purposes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premiums for insurance obtained by a Planned Unit Development (PUD), condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeowners association or Cooperative Corporation for the benefit of the PUD,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project or Cooperative Project will be paid as a common expense apportioned
elements.label: text

elements.text: to each unit owner or Shareholder.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Condominium Project is located in Puerto Rico, premiums for insurance for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium homeowners’ association policy for the benefit of the Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be paid by the Servicer if the Servicer collects Escrow for such purposes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8202.1: General property insurance requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (Future effective date 01/01/27)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General property insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum insurance monitoring requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance charges covering the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annual insurance reminder
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General property insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For as long as Freddie Mac owns a Mortgage, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: I. Ensure that the Mortgaged Premises is covered by insurance meeting the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapters 4703 and 8202, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: II. Ensure property and flood, when applicable, insurance premiums are paid, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: III. Obtain Lender-Placed Insurance (LPI) when required in accordance with applicable law,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Security Instrument and the Guide. See Section 8202.6 for LPI requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 4703.1 for requirements related to insurer licensing, assessments and ratings, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 4703.2 for minimum property insurance requirements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 4703.3 for flood insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgage clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must ensure all insurance policies documenting insurance coverage(s) obtained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Freddie Mac’s requirements for 1- to 4-unit properties include the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: industry’s standard Mortgage clause and meet the requirements outlined in Section 4703.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Minimum insurance monitoring requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Freddie Mac-owned Mortgage it services, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Confirm, at least annually (or upon policy renewal or replacement), that the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is insured and the coverage meets or exceeds the minimum insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements outlined in Chapter 4703, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Have reasonable policies, procedures and controls in place to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: I. Confirm insurance policies meet the minimum requirements in Chapter 4703
elements.label: text

elements.text: II. If an initial insurance review cannot determine whether an insurance policy meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum requirements in Chapter 4703, obtain additional evidence of insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer must obtain additional evidence of 1- to 4-unit property insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage sufficiency, it may, as an alternative or supplement to other methods of
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidencing replacement cost, monitor the dwelling coverage limit for renewal
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies. If the dwelling coverage limit compared to the prior year’s policy has
elements.label: text

elements.text: decreased, then the Servicer may not consider this as additional evidence of coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In instances where reasonable efforts to obtain additional evidence that the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy meets the minimum requirements of Chapter 4703 have been exhausted and
elements.label: text

elements.text: failed, the Servicer must document its efforts in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: III. Notify the Borrower when the Servicer identifies a 1- to 4-unit property insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy that does not meet one or more of the following minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: outlined in Sections 4703.1 and 4703.2:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Carrier licensing, assessment and rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coverage sufficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Perils
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deductibles
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Insurance charges covering the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premiums for insurance covering the Mortgaged Premises will be paid when due by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers or the Servicer if the Servicer collects Escrows for such purposes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premiums for insurance obtained by a Planned Unit Development (PUD), condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeowners association or Cooperative Corporation for the benefit of the PUD,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project or Cooperative Project will be paid as a common expense apportioned
elements.label: text

elements.text: to each unit owner or Shareholder.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Condominium Project is located in Puerto Rico, premiums for insurance for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium homeowners’ association policy for the benefit of the Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be paid by the Servicer if the Servicer collects Escrow for such purposes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Annual insurance reminder
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least annually, Servicers must provide a reminder to Borrowers of their responsibility to
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain insurance on the Mortgaged Premises and recommend they contact their insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider to review their coverage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may include this reminder in any existing Borrower communication, refer the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to applicable insurance information on the Servicer’s website or direct the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to insurance resources on Freddie Mac’s website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may deliver this notice via any method as permitted by applicable law, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, e-mail, text messaging, voice response unit technology or a Servicer’s web
elements.label: text

elements.text: portal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8202.2: Flood insurance requirements (06/10/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For each Freddie Mac-owned Mortgage it services, the Servicer must have policies and controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: in place to ensure that the Mortgaged Premises, when required, has and maintains flood
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance coverage that meets the minimum requirements outlined in Section 4703.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have policies, procedures and controls in place to ensure the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequately protected by flood insurance when required, with no lapses in coverage, and the flood
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premiums are paid. If at any time during the term of the Mortgage the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is not covered by flood insurance in the amount and with the deductible required, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must follow the Lender-Placed Insurance (LPI) process under Section 8202.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have policies, procedures and controls in place to identify any map change
elements.label: text

elements.text: that becomes effective on or after April 1, 1995 and determine which insurable improvements on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in the community affected by the map change become located in a Special
elements.label: text

elements.text: Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) and are required to have flood insurance. Flood insurance required
elements.label: text

elements.text: under these provisions must be obtained within 120 days of the effective date of the map change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the area where the Mortgaged Premises is located is an SFHA but the community has become
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: claims and disburse insurance loss proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer responsibilities for insurance claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting damage to the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disbursing loss proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repair or reconstruction of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Final inspection of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer responsibilities for insurance claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon notification of loss or damage to the Mortgaged Premises, the Servicer must monitor
elements.label: text

elements.text: and coordinate the claim process with the Borrower and the insurer. The Servicer must take
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate action to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verify the extent of the loss or damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure judicious disbursement of insurance proceeds for the necessary repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protect the priority of the Mortgage by obtaining, where necessary, waivers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: materialman’s or mechanic’s liens
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document details concerning the loss or damage, completion of the repairs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: disposition of the insurance proceeds in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit payment of fees out of the insurance loss proceeds to any public adjuster or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: third party retained by the Borrower to assist with the recovery of those proceeds unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreed to by Freddie Mac in writing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refer to Chapter 8403 for additional requirements on Abandoned Properties (as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 8403.1), distressed properties or properties that pose a Risk of Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reporting damage to the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer does not need to submit a report and related recommendations to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises is abandoned and/or has been acquired by the Servicer through
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. (See Section 9603.1(d)(ii)) for remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for insurance loss settlements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The insured improvements have suffered a total or near total loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The insured improvements cannot be rebuilt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer wishes to apply insurance proceeds to the Mortgage debt instead of
elements.label: text

elements.text: repairing the property, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance proceeds exceed the amounts required to restore the property to its original
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of the above conditions exist, the Servicer must submit the recommendation along
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the appropriate documentation to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within five Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of learning of the situation.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: must release the insurance funds to the Borrower without delay.
